desc: Marks a CentralWarningLogger.WarningLogEntry as acknowledged.
summary: Marks a CentralWarningLogger.WarningLogEntry as acknowledged. After the AI presents a warning and the user confirms they've seen it, this command flags it so it doesn't reappear in unacknowledged filters.
precondition: Entry ID must exist in the central warning log.
postcondition: Entry.Acknowledged = true.

config_inputs.name: EntryId
config_inputs.description: GUID of the warning log entry to acknowledge

desc: Appends a timestamped comment to a clash row in the newest QA results XML (orchestrated parameters: clashId, comment).
summary: Appends a timestamped comment to a clash row in the newest QA results XML (orchestrated parameters: clashId, comment).

preprompts: Appending clash comment to QA XML…
thinkingsteps: Resolving latest results_*.xml beside the saved model…
Locating the clash row by id…

desc: Creates a detail line in the active view between two points specified in millimetres.
summary: Creates a detail line in the active view between two points specified in millimetres. Optionally applies a named line style via ResolveLineStyle. Coordinates are converted from mm to feet internally. Uses CommandExecutor to retrieve parameters from the AI orchestrator. Related: AddFilledRegionCommand for creating filled regions in the same view.
usecase: User wants to draw a detail line between two known coordinates. AI needs to add annotation lines to a drafting or detail view.
notfor: Creating model lines in 3D (use ModelLine API instead). Drawing multiple connected lines (use a polyline command or loop externally). Creating filled regions (use AddFilledRegionCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open with a detail-capable view (floor plan, section, drafting view). Coordinates x1, y1, x2, y2 must be provided in millimetres.
postcondition: A new DetailCurve is created in the active view. If a line style name was provided and found, it is applied to the new curve.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Detail line creation result with element ID

preprompts: Drawing a detail line in the active view...
thinkingsteps: Converting coordinates from mm to feet...
Creating detail curve in the active view...
successprompts: Done — detail line drawn between your two points.

desc: Creates a filled region in the active view from a set of XY points provided in millimetres.
summary: Creates a filled region in the active view from a set of XY points provided in millimetres. Points are parsed from a JSON array, converted to feet, and used to build a closed CurveLoop. An optional region type name can be specified; otherwise the first available FilledRegionType is used. Uses CommandExecutor to retrieve parameters from the AI orchestrator. Related: AddDetailLineCommand for drawing individual detail lines.
usecase: User wants to create a hatched or solid region to highlight an area on a plan or section. AI needs to annotate a view with a bounded filled region.
notfor: Creating 3D floor slabs or model regions (use Floor or ModelLine API). Drawing open polylines (use AddDetailLineCommand in a loop).
precondition: Active document must be open with a detail-capable view. At least 3 XY points must be provided in mm as a JSON array: [[x,y],[x,y],...].
postcondition: A new FilledRegion element is created in the active view using the resolved or default region type.

config_inputs.name: points
config_inputs.description: JSON array of XY points in mm: [[x,y],[x,y],...]

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Filled region creation result with element ID

preprompts: Creating a filled region in the active view...
thinkingsteps: Building curve loop and creating filled region...
successprompts: Done — filled region drawn with {Result} boundary.

desc: Adds an existing view filter to a view template by name.
summary: Adds an existing view filter to a view template by name. Resolves the template via ListTemplates.GetByName and the filter via ListFilters.GetByName. Delegates the actual filter addition to AddFilterToTemplate. Visibility of the filter on the template can be controlled via the 'visible' parameter. Related: ApplyViewTemplateCommand for applying a template to a view.
usecase: User wants to add a discipline-specific filter to a standard template. AI is configuring view templates as part of automated sheet setup.
notfor: Creating new filters (use a dedicated CreateFilterCommand). Applying templates to views (use ApplyViewTemplateCommand). Removing filters from templates.
precondition: Active document must be open. Both the target view template and the filter must already exist in the project.
postcondition: The specified filter is added to the template's filter list with the given visibility setting. If the filter was already present, it remains (idempotent).

preprompts: Adding filter to the view template...
Assigning the filter to your template now...
thinkingsteps: Adding filter to template with visibility setting...
successprompts: Done — filter is now on the template.
failureprompts: Could not add filter to template: {error}

desc: Adds an existing view filter to a regular (non-template) view identified by name.
summary: Adds an existing view filter to a regular (non-template) view identified by name. Resolves the view via ListViews.FindByExactName and delegates to AddFilterToView.AddFilter(Autodesk.Revit.DB.View, string, bool).
usecase: Turn on a discipline filter on a working floor plan or section for QA checks.
notfor: View templates — use AddFilterToTemplateCommand.
precondition: Active document; filterName and viewName parameters supplied.
postcondition: Filter is present on the view with the requested visibility when the helper returns true.

config_inputs.name: viewName
config_inputs.description: Exact name of the target view (not a template)

outputs.name: viewId
outputs.description: Target view element id when successful

preprompts: Applying the filter to your view...
Resolving the target view and filter...
Checking view type (must not be a template)...
thinkingsteps: Read viewName, filterName, and optional visible from command parameters.
Resolve the view with ListViews.FindByExactName (case-insensitive exact match).
Reject template views; suggest AddFilterToTemplateCommand if IsTemplate.
Parse visible; recover to true if the value is not a valid boolean.
Start a manual transaction, call AddFilterToView.AddFilter(Autodesk.Revit.DB.View, string, bool).
Commit on success; roll back when the helper returns false.
successprompts: Done — the view now includes the requested filter.
failureprompts: Could not add filter to view: {error}

desc: Adds an existing Revision to a target ViewSheet's additional revisions list.
summary: Adds an existing Revision to a target ViewSheet's additional revisions list. The sheet can be resolved by sheet number (preferred) or by element ID. Uses CommandExecutor to retrieve parameters from the AI orchestrator. If the revision is already present on the sheet, the operation is idempotent. Related: ApplyViewTemplateCommand for template-level view configuration.
usecase: User wants to mark a sheet as part of a specific revision issue. AI is updating multiple sheets with a new revision during drawing issue preparation.
notfor: Creating new revisions (use Revision.Create or a dedicated CreateRevisionCommand). Removing revisions from sheets. Managing revision clouds or tags.
precondition: Active document must be open. The target revision element must exist in the project. Either sheetNumber or sheetId must be provided to resolve the target sheet.
postcondition: The revision is added to the sheet's additional revisions list. If already present, the list remains unchanged (idempotent).

config_inputs.name: sheetId
config_inputs.description: Sheet element id (optional if sheetNumber provided)

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Result summary with sheet and revision details

preprompts: Adding revision to the target sheet...
Assigning the revision to your sheet now...
thinkingsteps: Resolving target sheet by number or ID...
successprompts: Done — sheet now includes the specified revision.
failureprompts: Could not add revision to sheet: {error}

desc: Distribute Dimensions vertically with a uniform spacing.
summary: Distribute Dimensions vertically with a uniform spacing.
usecase: Drawing presentation — uniform stacking of dimensions.
notfor: Aligning to a reference line (use AlignDimensionsToReferenceLineCommand when built).
precondition: Active document open; at least one dimension id supplied; spacing > 0.
postcondition: Every input dimension moved by (index × spacing) on Y. One committed Transaction.
logictree: 1) Resolver_DimensionIdsCsv → list of ids; Resolver_SpacingMm (default 300). 2) Resolve dimensions (preserving input order so spacing is predictable). 3) Validate spacing > 0 and ≥ 1 dimension. Else WireError. 4) Open Transaction 'Distribute dimensions'. 5) For i = 0..N-1 move element i by (0, i × spacingFt, 0). 6) Commit. 7) Bot JSON / User toast. 8) Exception → tracker.Fail + WireFault.

desc: Merge adjacent dimension strings into one, preserving every anchor Reference.
summary: Merge adjacent dimension strings into one, preserving every anchor Reference.
usecase: Cleaning up multiple collinear dimensions into a single running dimension for drawing presentation.
notfor: Non-collinear / disjoint dimension groups.
precondition: Active document open; active view is a View; at least two Dimension ids supplied.
postcondition: One new Dimension added; input Dimensions deleted. One committed Transaction.
logictree: 1) Resolver_DimensionIdsCsv → list of ids (≥ 2). 2) Resolve each to a Dimension on the active view; validate at least two survive. Else WireError + abort. 3) Open Transaction 'Merge dimension strings'. 4) Order by curve start-X; collect each dimension's References into a single ReferenceArray; pick the first dimension's line as the merged dimension line. 5) merged = doc.Create.NewDimension(view, line, refs). 6) Delete every original dimension. 7) Commit. 8) Bot JSON / User toast. 9) Exception → tracker.Fail + WireFault.

thinkingsteps: Creating merged dimension and deleting originals…

summary: Seeds default arrowheads and tag families per the Annotation Preferences Plan. Walks each known category, attempts to resolve via the three-tier resolver, and downloads from Supabase if absent. Idempotent — safe to re-run.
usecase: User opens a fresh model and wants every tagging command to "just work" without picking families.
notfor: Loading a single specific family (use AnnotationPreferencesDownloadFamilyFromSupabase directly).
precondition: Active document must be open. Internet access required for Supabase fallback.
postcondition: The standard MEP tag and arrowhead families are loaded into the active document.

preprompts: Seeding standard MEP tag and arrowhead families…
thinkingsteps: Checking which annotation families are already loaded…
successprompts: Loaded {LoadedCount} standard annotation families. {SkippedCount} already present.

desc: Split a Dimension at a supplied point on its curve.
summary: Split a Dimension at a supplied point on its curve.
usecase: Visual cleanup — breaking a long running dimension into per-area strings.
notfor: Non-linear dimension curves.
precondition: Active document open; dimension id resolves to a Dimension with a linear curve; split point projects cleanly onto it and produces two sides each with ≥ 2 references.
postcondition: Original dimension deleted; two new dimensions exist. One committed Transaction.
logictree: 1) Resolvers: Resolver_DimensionId, Resolver_Split(X,Y,Z) in feet. 2) Resolve dimension; verify curve is a Line; project split point onto line. 3) Transaction 'Split dimension string'. 4) Partition References by bbox-centroid X vs. projected X into leftRefs / rightRefs; each side must have ≥ 2 references. 5) NewDimension(left) + NewDimension(right); delete original. 6) Commit. 7) Bot JSON / User toast. 8) Exception → tracker.Fail + WireFault.

thinkingsteps: Projecting split point onto dimension line…

desc: Command: Applies linked model display settings from current view's template to all templates.
summary: Command: Applies linked model display settings from current view's template to all templates. WORKFLOW: 1. Gets the current active view 2. Gets the view template applied to that view 3. Extracts all RevitLinkInstance visibility/graphics overrides from the template 4. Applies those overrides to all other view templates in the document 5. Reports success with count of updated templates
usecase: User has set up linked model visibility in one template and wants to match all others. User wants consistent linked model display across all views. User needs to propagate architectural link settings to all MEP templates.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open with at least one view template. Current view should have a view template assigned. At least one RevitLinkInstance should exist in the model.
postcondition: All view templates in the document have the same linked model display settings as the source template from the current view.
sideeffects: Modifies all view templates in the document. Changes RevitLinkInstance visibility/graphics settings in templates.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continuecheck: activeView.hasTemplate → continue, else → erroraction: getSourceTemplate → from activeView.ViewTemplateIdaction: getLinkedInstances → collect RevitLinkInstancesaction: getSourceSettings → GetLinkOverrides for each linkaction: getAllTemplates → ListTemplates.GetAllaction: applyToAllTemplates → SetLinkOverrides for each templateresolve: success → showSuccess with count

selection_inputs.name: SourceView
selection_inputs.description: Source view with template to copy from
selection_inputs.prompt: Active view is used as source

config_inputs.name: ApplyToAllTemplates
config_inputs.description: Whether to apply to all templates or same type only

desc: Applies a named view template to a target view using SetTemplate.ApplyTemplate.
summary: Applies a named view template to a target view using SetTemplate.ApplyTemplate. If no viewId is provided, the active view is used as the target. The template is resolved by exact name match against all view templates in the project. Related: AddFilterToTemplateCommand for adding filters to templates before applying them.
usecase: User wants to apply a standard template to the current view for consistent display. AI is configuring views as part of automated sheet setup or coordination workflows.
notfor: Creating new view templates (use a dedicated CreateViewTemplateCommand). Adding filters to templates (use AddFilterToTemplateCommand). Removing templates from views.
precondition: Active document must be open. The named view template must exist in the project. The target view must not itself be a template (cannot apply a template to a template).
postcondition: The target view's ViewTemplateId is set to the resolved template's element ID. All template-controlled settings are applied to the view.

config_inputs.name: viewId
config_inputs.description: Optional target view id (defaults to active view)

outputs.name: Result
outputs.description: Apply template result with view and template details

preprompts: Applying view template to the target view...
Setting up the view template now...
successprompts: Template '{templateName}' applied to the view successfully.
Done — view now uses the specified template.

desc: Create an elevation view of a selected wall.
summary: Create an elevation view of a selected wall.
usecase: Wall elevation sheets for tile / cladding / IPS layouts.
notfor: Interior elevations requiring rooms (use the room-elevation command when built).
precondition: Active document open; wall id resolves; parent plan view exists or active view is a ViewPlan; project has an Elevation ViewFamilyType.
postcondition: One new ElevationMarker + one Elevation View added. One committed Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) Resolvers: Resolver_WallId (required), Resolver_ParentViewId (optional, default=active plan view), Resolver_StandoffMm (default 1000). 2) Validate — wall id → Wall; parent view id → ViewPlan; else WireError + abort. 3) Compute marker placement via GetElevationMarkerPlacementFromWall.Compute(wall, standoffFt). If null → abort. 4) Find Elevation ViewFamilyType id; if none → WireError + abort. 5) Open Transaction 'Create elevation of wall'. 6) marker = ElevationMarker.CreateElevationMarker(doc, vftId, origin, scale). 7) view = marker.CreateElevation(doc, parentView.Id, 0). Rotate marker to face the wall if necessary via ElementTransformUtils.RotateElement. 8) Commit. 9) Bot: JSON {ok, marker_id, view_id, wall_id}. User: toast 'Elevation view '{view.Name}' created.' 10) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {wall_id, parent_view_id, standoff_mm}.

preprompts: Creating elevation view of selected wall…

desc: Materialises an EnclosureRectangle as four walls on a specified Level + WallType.
summary: Materialises an EnclosureRectangle as four walls on a specified Level + WallType. Pure rectangle maths are upstream.
usecase: Materialise an enclosure rectangle (IPS, services-boxing, beam boxing) in one atomic step.
notfor: Computing the rectangle itself (use GetEnclosureRectangle).
precondition: Active document open; Level exists; WallType resolves (or default-wall-type is available); height > 0; four corners present.
postcondition: Four Wall elements added to the model inside one committed Transaction. Their ids returned in tracker output.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies four corner XYZ values (feet) + Resolver_HeightMm + Resolver_LevelId + Resolver_WallTypeId?. 2) Validate preconditions — level exists; height > 0; wallTypeId valid (fall back to Doc.GetDefaultElementTypeId(ElementTypeGroup.WallType) only if Resolver_WallTypeId is null — never silently). 3) Open Transaction 'Create enclosure walls'. 4) For each of (A→B, B→C, C→D, D→A) call Wall.Create(doc, Line.CreateBound(p0_on_level, p1_on_level), wallTypeId, levelId, heightFt, 0, false, false) and collect the result. 5) If any Wall.Create returns null → tracker.Fail + WireError + rollback. 6) Commit. 7) Bot: JSON {ok, wall_ids, level_id, height_mm}. User: toast 'Placed 4 enclosure walls on level {level.Name}.' 8) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {corners, level_id, height_mm, wall_type_id}.

preprompts: Placing four walls around enclosure rectangle…
thinkingsteps: Creating Wall A→B, B→C, C→D, D→A…

desc: Create a ViewSchedule for a category and add the supplied fields.
summary: Create a ViewSchedule for a category and add the supplied fields.
usecase: Architectural door / window / furniture schedule creation — or any bespoke category schedule with custom fields.
notfor: Editing an existing ViewSchedule (use the dedicated edit commands).
precondition: Active document open; category is a valid BuiltInCategory int value; field_names non-empty; project has at least one element in the category (Revit will still create an empty schedule, but we warn via WireRecoverable).
postcondition: Exactly one new ViewSchedule added. One committed Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies Resolver_CategoryIntValue (BuiltInCategory cast to int), Resolver_ScheduleName, Resolver_FieldNamesCsv. 2) Validate — category id is a known BuiltInCategory; schedule name non-empty; at least one field name. Else WireError + abort. 3) Open Transaction 'Create category schedule'. 4) schedule = ViewSchedule.CreateSchedule(doc, new ElementId(category)); try set schedule.Name (swallow NameClash with WireRecoverable). 5) fields = GetScheduleFieldsByName.Find(doc, schedule.Definition, splitCsv). 6) For each field: schedule.Definition.AddField(field). 7) Commit. 8) Bot: JSON {ok, schedule_id, schedule_name, added_field_count}. User: toast 'Schedule {schedule.Name} created with {n} field(s).' 9) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {category, schedule_name, field_count}.

preprompts: Creating schedule for category with field list…
thinkingsteps: Resolving schedulable fields for supplied names…

desc: Create Walls from a batch of ModelCurve element ids at a given Level.
summary: Create Walls from a batch of ModelCurve element ids at a given Level.
usecase: Batch wall tracing from CAD-traced curves or detail lines.
notfor: Creating a single wall manually (use Revit's native Wall tool).
precondition: Active document open; level id resolves; at least one curve id resolves to a ModelCurve / DetailLine / Line-like element.
postcondition: N Walls added where N = usable curves. Skipped count returned. One committed Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) Resolvers: Resolver_CurveElementIdsCsv, Resolver_LevelId, Resolver_WallTypeId?, Resolver_HeightMm (default 3000). 2) Validate — level id resolves to a Level; height > 0; at least one curve id present. Else WireError + abort. 3) Resolve wall type id (explicit → validate; else doc.GetDefaultElementTypeId(WallType); if none → WireError + abort). 4) Open Transaction 'Create walls from curves'. 5) For each curve id: resolve element; if not a ModelCurve-like → WireRecoverable + skipped++. Otherwise flatten via FlattenCurveToLevelElevation.ToZ(curve, level.Elevation); if null → WireRecoverable + skipped++; else Wall.Create and accumulate id. 6) Commit. 7) Bot: JSON {ok, wall_ids, skipped, level_id, height_mm}. User: toast 'Created {n} wall(s) (skipped {m}).' 8) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault payload.

thinkingsteps: Flattening each curve to level elevation…

desc: Places a Dimension between the edge of a source element and the centreline reference of a target Wall, in the active plan view.
summary: Places a Dimension between the edge of a source element and the centreline reference of a target Wall, in the active plan view. Pure-logic edge picking is delegated to GetElementEdges.
usecase: Architecture coordination — engineer wants the distance from a door / window / grid / MEP-family to a named wall, dimensioned to drawing standards.
notfor: Face-to-face dimensions (use `ArchitectureDimensionFromElementFaceToWallFaceCommand` when built). Not for 3D views.
precondition: Active document open; active view is a ViewPlan; selection contains one source element + one Wall, or both IDs supplied via resolvers.
postcondition: Exactly one new Dimension added to the active view, inside a single committed Transaction, audit trail recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot picks source element + wall (resolvers: Resolver_SourceElementId, Resolver_WallId, Resolver_OffsetMm default=300). 2) Validate preconditions — Active view is ViewPlan; source element and wall exist in Doc; wall has a linear LocationCurve; else ErrorWire.WireError with reason and abort. 3) Open Transaction "Dim edge → wall". 4) Call GetElementEdges.GetReferencesForDimension(Doc, [source, wall]) to obtain ReferenceArray; if count < 2 WireError + abort. 5) Compute the dimension line: midpoint between source BB centre and wall centreline, offset perpendicular to the wall centreline by Resolver_OffsetMm. 6) doc.Create.NewDimension(activeView, dimLine, refArray); commit Transaction. 7) Bot: return JSON {ok, dimension_id, value_mm, source_element_id, wall_id, view_id}. User: ribbon toast "Dimension placed — {value_mm} mm." 8) Any exception during resolving / computing / creating → tracker.Fail(ex); ErrorWire.WireFault with structured payload {source_id, wall_id, view_id, offset_mm}. Transaction rolls back.

preprompts: Placing dimension from selected element edge to wall…
thinkingsteps: Placing Dimension in active plan view…
successprompts: Dimension placed — {value_mm} mm between element {source_id} and wall {wall_id}.

desc: Splits a column FamilyInstance into two segments at a supplied Level.
summary: Splits a column FamilyInstance into two segments at a supplied Level. Pure validation and level-lookup delegated to GetColumnBaseAndTopLevels.
usecase: Convert floor-to-floor-to-floor single-column modelling into per-level segments for schedule / QA.
notfor: Structural splits that require axis changes or rotations (use the structural variant, not yet built).
precondition: Active document open; column id resolves to a FamilyInstance with FAMILY_BASE_LEVEL_PARAM + FAMILY_TOP_LEVEL_PARAM; split level id resolves to a Level strictly between base and top.
postcondition: One additional column exists in the model; the original now spans base→split and the copy spans split→top. One committed Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies Resolver_ColumnId + Resolver_SplitLevelId. 2) Resolve both — the element must be a FamilyInstance whose category is OST_Columns or OST_StructuralColumns; the split level must be a Level. Else WireError + abort. 3) Read bounds via GetColumnBaseAndTopLevels.Read(doc, column); if null → abort. 4) Validate via GetColumnBaseAndTopLevels.ValidateSplitLevel(bounds, splitLevel); if false → WireError 'split_level_not_between_base_and_top' + abort. 5) Open Transaction 'Split column at level'. 6) Set original column's TOP_LEVEL_PARAM to splitLevelId (now original = bottom segment). 7) Copy via ElementTransformUtils.CopyElement(doc, column.Id, XYZ.Zero); set copy's BASE_LEVEL_PARAM = splitLevelId and TOP_LEVEL_PARAM = original top level id. 8) Commit. 9) Bot: JSON {ok, bottom_column_id, top_column_id, split_level_id}. User: toast 'Column split at '{level.Name}'.' 10) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {column_id, split_level_id}.

thinkingsteps: Validating split level is strictly between…
successprompts: Column split at '{level_name}'. Bottom id {bottom_column_id}, top id {top_column_id}.

desc: Applies default Revit resolution to warnings that have available auto-fixes.
summary: Applies default Revit resolution to warnings that have available auto-fixes. Use MCP.GetWarningsWithAvailableAutoResolutions to preview first. IMPORTANT: Some resolutions DELETE elements — always describe the fix to user first.
precondition: Active document must have warnings with available resolutions.

By default writes results to .mepbridge/qa/results_*.xml for the QA Manager. When outputMode=json is passed, returns lightweight JSON instead (MCP path).

desc: Clash detection between linked model elements (e.g.
summary: Clash detection between linked model elements (e.g. structural beams, columns) and host MEP services (pipes, ducts, cable trays). Delegates to ClashLinkedWithHost.GetAll for the intersection logic. Returns hostElementIds that can be passed to SectionBoxAroundClashesCommand to zoom to specific clashes. Use CheckClashesCommand for host-only (non-linked) clashes.
usecase: Structural vs MEP clash check before coordination meeting. Identifying where pipes/ducts clash with structural beams or columns. Generating clash reports for BIM coordination.
notfor: Host-only clashes (use CheckClashesCommand). Resolving clashes automatically. Mechanical vs electrical clashes within host model only.
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one linked model must be loaded. MEP elements must exist in the host model.
postcondition: Result JSON stored in LastQueryResult with clash pairs, overlap bounds, and hostElementIds. Optional XML report written to disk if outputXml path is provided. No model modifications — read-only analysis.

desc: ClashFinderImportResultsFromNavisworksXmlCommand — stage an NW export XML into the staging DB.

usecase: NW→Revit data bridge.
notfor: Re-running detection — this is purely ingestion.
precondition: Resolver_FilePath points to a valid NW exchange XML.
postcondition: Zero or more ClashRecord upserts.
logictree:
1) Resolver_FilePath required; file must exist.
2) Read text + delegate parsing to ImportClashesFromNavisworksFile.Read.
3) For each row, build ClashRecord with id = row.Id (or SHA-256 over rule + sorted guids if blank); state already normalised by the reader.
4) ClashesRepository.Upsert; count inserts vs updates.
5) Emit imported + upserted + skipped.

desc: ClashFinderRunByFilterPairsWithTolerancesCommand — auto-generated CMD skeleton.
summary: ClashFinderRunByFilterPairsWithTolerancesCommand — auto-generated CMD skeleton. Fill workflow body per plan §8.
usecase: Deterministic action per the workflow below — User or Bot path.
notfor: Read-only queries (use the matching MCP tool).
precondition: Active document must be open. User has selection or resolvers are filled.
postcondition: Model state changed inside a Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot selects target/context (resolvers filled from chat input or UI). 2) Validate preconditions; WireError + abort if missing required state. 3) Open Transaction, perform deterministic steps, WireRecoverable on any fallback. 4) Bot → MCP success summary. User → ribbon toast + chat + audit trail. 5) Failure on either path → `ErrorWire.WireFault` + `CentralWarningLogger.LogError` with structured payload.

postcondition: Clashes upserted per workset pair.

1) If doc is not workshared → WireError.
2) Resolver_WorksetPairs default to [(MEP|Mechanical|Electrical|Plumbing, Structural|Structure)].
3) For each pair: resolve WorksetId by name match; collect elements per side via ElementWorksetFilter.
4) Delegate pipeline.Run.
5) Aggregate + emit summary.

desc: ClashFinderRunOnSelectionAgainstRestOfModelCommand — selection vs rest-of-model pipeline invocation.
summary: ClashFinderRunOnSelectionAgainstRestOfModelCommand — selection vs rest-of-model pipeline invocation.
usecase: Targeted "does this new run clash?" probe.
notfor: Full model or view-scoped — use the other Run commands.
precondition: Active document; selection (resolver or active) of ≥ 1 element.
postcondition: Clashes upserted for pairs (selection × rest).
logictree:
1) ids = Resolver_ElementIds OR current selection (active view). WireError if empty.
2) set_a = resolve ids → Elements; drop nulls.
3) set_b = all MEPCurves + MechanicalEquipment in doc minus set_a ids.
4) pipeline.Run(doc, "selection_vs_rest", set_a, set_b).
5) Emit per_pair + total_upserted.

desc: ClashFinderRunOnVisibleElementsInCurrentViewCommand — view-scoped pipeline invocation.

notfor: Full-model detection — use the main Run command.
precondition: Active document + active view; at least one MEP category populated in the view.
postcondition: Clashes upserted into the staging DB per category pair in-view.
logictree:
1) view = Resolver_ViewId-resolved OR active view.
2) Iterate canonical category pairs (same set as main Run command).
3) Set A = FilteredElementCollector(doc, view.Id).OfCategory(catA).WhereElementIsNotElementType().
4) Set B = FilteredElementCollector(doc, view.Id).OfCategory(catB).WhereElementIsNotElementType().
5) Delegate pipeline.Run(doc, rule, setA, setB).
6) Aggregate + emit per_pair summary + totals.

desc: ClashManagerAddCommentToResultCommand — append a timestamped comment to a clash's `comment` column via the staging repo.
summary: ClashManagerAddCommentToResultCommand — append a timestamped comment to a clash's `comment` column via the staging repo.
usecase: QA review comment append.
notfor: Changing state or fix_status — use the dedicated commands.
precondition: Resolver_ClashId resolvable in staging DB; Resolver_Comment non-empty.
postcondition: Single SQLite UPDATE inside the repo; no Revit Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ClashId + Resolver_Comment required. WireError otherwise.
2) Load ClashRecord; WireError if missing.
3) Build appended_line = "[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ] [author] comment".
4) Call repo.TransitionState(id, current_state, comment=new_comment) — repo merges into existing comment.
5) Emit ok + appended_line.

2) Resolve pair; WireError on missing.

desc: ClashManagerCycleThroughAllResultsInOrderCommand — advance the QA cursor by one, navigate, return context.
summary: ClashManagerCycleThroughAllResultsInOrderCommand — advance the QA cursor by one, navigate, return context.
usecase: QA walk-through of the clash list.
notfor: Bulk export — use the Export commands.
precondition: Active document; ≥ 1 clash in staging DB matching state filter.
postcondition: Active view set to a 3D view with section box around the next pair.
logictree:
1) state_filter = Resolver_StateFilter ?? "active". If "active" yields zero rows, fall back to "new".
2) Snapshot the id list; if the current process-scoped cursor refers to an id not in this list (or Resolver_Reset=true), reset cursor to 0 and set wrapped=true.
3) Pick clash at cursor; increment cursor (wraps at end, emits wrapped=true on wrap).
4) Resolve pair in doc; WireError if missing.
5) CycleViewToNextClashResult.ZoomTo(uidoc, [a,b], margin_ft).
6) Emit { clash_id, index, total, wrapped, view_id }.

desc: ClashManagerExportResultsToHtmlReportCommand — self-contained HTML report exporter (images inlined).
summary: ClashManagerExportResultsToHtmlReportCommand — self-contained HTML report exporter (images inlined).

desc: ClashManagerExportResultsToNavisworksXmlCommand — write an NW-compatible exchange XML from the staging clashes table.
summary: ClashManagerExportResultsToNavisworksXmlCommand — write an NW-compatible exchange XML from the staging clashes table.
usecase: Hand-off to Navisworks Manage coordinator.
notfor: Reading NW XML — use ClashFinderImportResultsFromNavisworksXmlCommand.
precondition: Active doc; staging DB has ≥ 1 clash row.
postcondition: Single .xml file on disk.
logictree:
1) Resolve output path (default .mepbridge/qa/reports/clashes_{ts}.xml).
2) Pull rows via repo.ListByState(state_filter ?? "*").
3) Build XDocument: <exchange><batchtest><clashtest><clashresults><clashresult><description>...</description><clashpoint><pos3f .../></clashpoint><objects><object ...>...</object></objects></clashresult>...</clashresults></clashtest></batchtest></exchange>.
4) Pull element centroids for clashpoint; elements as <object> with guid + displayname.
5) Save XML with indentation; emit result summary.

desc: ClashManagerNavigateToResultInActiveViewCommand — snap a 3D section box around a clash pair and show the elements.
summary: ClashManagerNavigateToResultInActiveViewCommand — snap a 3D section box around a clash pair and show the elements.

1) Resolver_ClashId required; load ClashRecord.
2) Resolve UidA + UidB in doc; WireError on missing.
3) View3D = current if it's a View3D; else pick first non-template View3D. If none, WireError.
4) Call Create3DSectionBox.FromElements(doc, view3d, [a,b], margin_ft).
5) uidoc.ActiveView = view3d; uidoc.ShowElements([a.Id, b.Id]); uidoc.Selection.SetElementIds([a.Id, b.Id]).
6) Emit ok + view_id + element_ids.

desc: ClashManagerUpdateResultStatusCommand — sole state-transition write point for the `clashes.state` column.
summary: ClashManagerUpdateResultStatusCommand — sole state-transition write point for the `clashes.state` column.
usecase: QA workflow progression.

1) Resolver_ClashId + Resolver_ToState required; validate to_state ∈ {new, active, reviewed, resolved, ignored}.
2) Load row; compare from_state to enforce a valid transition path (ValidTransitions table).
3) repo.TransitionState(id, to_state, comment?).
4) Emit ok + from/to.

desc: ClashSolverAssessClashPostMoveCommand — PDF "ASSESS_CLASH".
summary: ClashSolverAssessClashPostMoveCommand — PDF "ASSESS_CLASH". After a move, verify the originating clash is gone and no new clashes were introduced on the moved element.
usecase: Single gate used after Resolve* commits or a human manual move.
notfor: Reverting a move — that's Restore state.
precondition: Active document; clash id + moved element resolvable.
postcondition: No mutation; tracker + repo state updated if auto-resolved.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ClashId required; fetch ClashRecord; WireError if missing.
2) Resolver_MovedUniqueId (Bot) OR single selection (Human); WireError if missing.
3) Resolve UidA + UidB in the doc; WireError if either gone.
4) clash_still_active = ElementIntersectsElementFilter(A→B) returns a hit.
5) new_clash_count = ForecastClashesAfterMove.ForecastMove(doc, moved, XYZ.Zero).ClashCount,
   excluding the original pair (that's counted by step 4).
6) If clash_still_active == false && new_clash_count == 0 → repo.TransitionState(resolved).
   Else emit detail for human review; do NOT auto-transition.
7) Tracker outputs + Human TaskDialog when on that path.

Running zero-move forecast on the moved element…

desc: ClashSolverAssessInterferenceOfBoundingBoxesCommand — PDF "ASSESS_INTERFERENCE".
summary: ClashSolverAssessInterferenceOfBoundingBoxesCommand — PDF "ASSESS_INTERFERENCE". Pairwise BB overlap between two elements.
usecase: Pre/post-move interference check driven by a resolver step.
notfor: Running a full category-pair clash pass — that's ClashFinderRunByFilterPairsWithTolerancesCommand.
precondition: Active document; uidA + uidB resolvers or exactly two selected elements.
postcondition: No mutation; audit recorded.
logictree:
1) Resolve pair — Resolver_UidA + Resolver_UidB if Bot; else the two currently selected elements (Human).
2) WireError if pair cannot be resolved or they are the same element.
3) Read BoundingBoxXYZ of each via Element.get_BoundingBox(null); WireError if either is null.
4) Call CheckBoundingBoxOverlap.AxisAligned(bbA, bbB, tolerance_mm) by default,
   or OrientedSat(bbA, Transform.Identity, bbB, Transform.Identity, tolerance_mm) if Resolver_UseSat.
5) Emit { overlaps, volume_mm3, reason, tolerance_mm, uidA, uidB, mode }.
6) Human path: TaskDialog with the same fields.

preprompts: Assessing bounding-box interference for the pair…

desc: ClashSolverCheckAngle90ThroughWallCloseIssueCommand — PDF "IF_ANGLE_IS_90_CLOSE_ISSUE".
summary: ClashSolverCheckAngle90ThroughWallCloseIssueCommand — PDF "IF_ANGLE_IS_90_CLOSE_ISSUE". Classify and auto-close qualifying perpendicular-through-wall clashes.
usecase: Auto-close compliant perpendicular wall penetrations without human click-per-clash.
notfor: Non-wall clashes — the helper will emit reason='not_wall_service_pair'.
precondition: Active document; Resolver_ClashId resolves in staging DB; pair still in the model.
postcondition: No model geometry mutation; clashes table row may transition to state='ignored'.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ClashId required; load ClashRecord; WireError if missing.
2) Resolve UidA + UidB in doc; WireError if either missing.
3) Call KeepAngleSteppedRouteOptions.Classify(a, b, angle_tolerance_deg).
4) If verdict.IsPerpendicular && verdict.FullyThroughWall →
     repo.TransitionState(clashId, 'ignored', "angle_90_through_wall: angle={angle_deg} deg, penetration={pen_ft} ft.").
   Else: no transition; emit diagnostic.
5) Tracker outputs + TaskDialog on Human path.
6) Never throws on a reason-based no-op; only WireFault on exception.

desc: ClashSolverCheckAvailableSpaceInAllDirectionsCommand — PDF "CHECK_AVAILABLE_SPACE".
summary: ClashSolverCheckAvailableSpaceInAllDirectionsCommand — PDF "CHECK_AVAILABLE_SPACE". Horizontal ±X/±Y clearance at a point compared against a pass threshold.
usecase: Pass/fail gate before committing a lateral move.
notfor: Actual movement.
precondition: Active document; either (x,y,z) resolvers, or one selected element.
postcondition: No mutation; audit recorded; pass/fail emitted.
logictree:
1) Resolve probe point: (Resolver_X, Resolver_Y, Resolver_Z) OR BB centre of one selected element.
2) WireError if no valid point.
3) threshold_mm = Resolver_ThresholdMm (required > 0).
4) Delegate to SpaceAnalysisCheckAvailableClearanceAtPoint.GetAll(doc, x, y, z, threshold_mm).
5) Compute pass = all four axes reported at_cap=true (i.e. nothing within threshold_mm).
6) Emit { probe, threshold_mm, result, pass, failing_axes }.

desc: ClashSolverConnectNodesWithin100mmToleranceCommand — PDF "CONNECT_INTO_NEAREST".
summary: ClashSolverConnectNodesWithin100mmToleranceCommand — PDF "CONNECT_INTO_NEAREST". Bulk-heal every unconnected connector pair whose Euclidean distance is ≤ tolerance_mm.
usecase: Bulk healing after batch moves.
notfor: Pair-level fine-grained connects — use the per-pair ConnectorOps commands.
precondition: Active document.
postcondition: Zero or more ConnectTo calls each inside their own Transaction so one failure doesn't roll back the batch.
logictree:
1) tolerance_mm = Resolver_ToleranceMm ?? 100.0.
2) Enumerate every connector in the doc that is IsConnected=false and not Logical. Collect into a list.
3) Produce candidate pairs (i, j) where i<j and Domain matches and distance <= tolerance_ft; sort by distance ascending.
4) Greedy consume: skip pairs whose connector has been used by an earlier accepted pair.
5) For each accepted pair, system-match gate. Skip if Abort; skip ProceedWithWarning unless allow_cross_system_with_warning=true.
6) Open a Transaction per pair; ConnectTo; Commit on success, RollBack + WireRecoverable on throw.
7) Aggregate per-pair outcomes; emit summary + per_pair list.

thinkingsteps: Running ConnectTo per pair (own Transaction each)…

desc: ClashSolverDetectThreadOfServicesAndCombineBoundingBoxesCommand — PDF "THREAD_OF_SERVICES".
summary: ClashSolverDetectThreadOfServicesAndCombineBoundingBoxesCommand — PDF "THREAD_OF_SERVICES". Identify parallel-run groups and return combined BBs.
usecase: Pre-step before any group-level solver move.
notfor: Mutating the model — pure grouping.
precondition: Active document; input element ids (resolver or selection); each element has a BoundingBox.
postcondition: No mutation; audit records threads + unthreaded.
logictree:
1) Resolve input ids: Resolver_ElementIds (Bot) else active selection (Human).
2) WireError if fewer than 2 element ids (nothing to thread).
3) angle_tol_deg = Resolver_AngleTolDeg ?? 5.0; lateral_tol_mm = Resolver_LateralTolMm ?? 300.0.
4) Call CombineBoundingBoxesSafely.Combine(doc, ids, angle, lateral).
5) Serialise threads + unthreaded into tracker outputs.
6) Human path: TaskDialog summary "N threads, X unthreaded".

desc: ClashSolverExtendAllUnusedConnectorsBy100mmCommand — PDF "EXTEND_CONNECTORS".
summary: ClashSolverExtendAllUnusedConnectorsBy100mmCommand — PDF "EXTEND_CONNECTORS". Thin wrapper over ExtendAllConnectors for the clash-solver audit trail.
usecase: Prepare end-runs before the resolve step calls CONNECT_INTO_NEAREST.
notfor: Bulk operations across the whole model — run once per element.
precondition: Active document; target element resolvable; has MEP connectors.
postcondition: New pipes created inside one Transaction; tracker records summary.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ElementUniqueId (Bot) OR single selection (Human); WireError if absent.
2) Resolve element; WireError if not resolvable.
3) length_mm = Resolver_LengthMm ?? 100.0; compute length_ft = length_mm/304.8.
4) Open Transaction "ClashSolver::ExtendConnectors"; new ExtendAllConnectors(doc, el)
   with ExtensionLength=length_ft + OnlyUnconnected=true; helper.Execute().
5) Commit iff any pipes created; otherwise RollBack.
6) Emit connectors_processed + pipes_created + created_pipe_ids.
keywords: solver prep for connect into nearest

desc: ClashSolverExtendSelectedNodeBy100mmCommand — PDF "EXTEND_NODE".
summary: ClashSolverExtendSelectedNodeBy100mmCommand — PDF "EXTEND_NODE". Extend one specific connector by 100 mm.
usecase: Prepare a single named end-run to meet the next node.
notfor: Extending every connector at once — use the other Extend* command.
precondition: Active document; element + connector id resolvable; connector is not already connected.
postcondition: Exactly one new Pipe created inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ElementUniqueId + Resolver_ConnectorId required.
2) Resolve element + ConnectorManager; fetch the connector by Id; WireError if any missing.
3) If connector.IsConnected → WireRecoverable + no-op + tracker output says already_connected.
4) length_mm = Resolver_LengthMm ?? 100; direction = connector.CoordinateSystem.BasisZ.
5) Resolve PipeTypeId + SystemTypeId + LevelId from the source element / connector.
6) Open Transaction "ClashSolver::ExtendNode"; Pipe.Create(doc, systemTypeId, pipeTypeId, levelId, origin, origin + dir*length); set diameter from connector; commit.
7) Emit created_pipe_id, length_mm, origin_ft, direction.

desc: ClashSolverFindNearestHostForDeviceCommand — PDF "FIND_NEAREST_HOST".
summary: ClashSolverFindNearestHostForDeviceCommand — PDF "FIND_NEAREST_HOST". Candidate host listing ordered by distance.
usecase: Pre-step before MOVE_TO_HOST / MOVE_TO_WALL.
notfor: Actually moving the device.
precondition: Active document; one FamilyInstance device resolvable.
postcondition: No mutation; audit recorded.
logictree:
1) Resolver_DeviceUniqueId required OR single selection. Must be a FamilyInstance.
2) Inspect fi.Host: if non-null, currentHostCategory = Host.Category; else fall back to HostBehavior.Plane / HostedByFace flag: tag as "any".
3) Build pool by BuiltInCategory matching currentHostCategory (Walls / Ceilings / Floors). If unknown, union Walls + Ceilings + Floors.
4) For each candidate, compute Euclidean distance between device.Location.Point and candidate BB centroid; skip beyond max radius.
5) Skip the device's current host.
6) Sort ascending; emit top 50 (configurable).

desc: ClashSolverIfAvailableMoveServiceIntoSpaceCommand — PDF "IF_AVAILABLE_MOVE_SERVICE_INTO_SPACE".
summary: ClashSolverIfAvailableMoveServiceIntoSpaceCommand — PDF "IF_AVAILABLE_MOVE_SERVICE_INTO_SPACE". Conditional guarded variant of MOVE_SERVICE_INTO_SPACE.
usecase: Solver probe step: "move only if there's somewhere to go".
notfor: Unconditional moves — use MoveServiceIntoDetectedSpace.
precondition: Active document; one service element resolvable; default 3D view available.
postcondition: Either a move (inner Transaction) or a no-op with a structured reason.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ElementUniqueId required OR single selection.
2) Probe point = LocationPoint else BB centroid.
3) CastRayFromPoint.CastSixDirections(max=threshold_mm/304.8).
4) available = any direction reports null hit (meaning cleared beyond cap) OR distance_mm ≥ threshold_mm.
5) If !available → emit { available=false, reason="no_direction_over_threshold" }. Exit without mutation.
6) If available → delegate to PlacementMoveElementIntoDetectedSpaceCommand with one-element scope + resolver overrides.
7) Emit { available=true, moved=true, delegated_to }.

desc: ClashSolverMoveDeviceHorizontallyIntoSpaceAlongWallCommand — PDF "MOVE_HORIZONTALLY_INTO_SPACE".

2) Resolve device; WireError if not FamilyInstance with Wall host.
3) wall = fi.Host as Wall; tangent = wall.LocationCurve tangent direction (projected onto XY).
4) If Resolver_PreferForward=false, flip tangent.
5) Delegate TryDirectionalMoveOnService.ProbeAndMove(doc, fi, tangent, step_ft, threshold_ft, centreFallback=false).
6) Emit outcome + wall_tangent_ft.

desc: ClashSolverMoveDeviceToNearestHostCommand — PDF "MOVE_TO_HOST".
summary: ClashSolverMoveDeviceToNearestHostCommand — PDF "MOVE_TO_HOST". Project device location onto the target host surface and move the device to the projection point.
usecase: Solver geometric re-host without changing Host reference.
notfor: Full host-reassignment — separate workflow (delete + recreate).
precondition: Active document; device + target host resolvable; device is FamilyInstance with LocationPoint; host category matches device's current host family.
postcondition: One MoveElement inside a single Transaction if projection succeeds.
logictree:
1) Resolve device (FamilyInstance) and targetHost (HostObject subclass: Wall, Ceiling, Floor, RoofBase).
2) WireError if device not FamilyInstance or host not HostObject.
3) Read device LocationPoint; WireError if missing.
4) Collect side faces of targetHost via HostObjectUtils.GetSideFaces (wall) / .GetBottomFaces (ceiling) / .GetTopFaces (floor).
5) Project device point onto every face; pick projection with smallest distance.
6) delta = projection - devicePoint. If delta zero → no-op.
7) Forecast gate with the delta; if introduces new clashes → WireError + no-op.
8) Transaction "ClashSolver::MoveToHost"; MoveElement(doc, device.Id, delta); commit.

preprompts: Projecting device onto target host surface…
successprompts: Moved device by {delta_ft} ft onto host.

desc: ClashSolverOffsetServiceAroundDuctBoundingBoxCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_AROUND_DUCT".
summary: ClashSolverOffsetServiceAroundDuctBoundingBoxCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_AROUND_DUCT". Offset the service by the duct's BB extent on the chosen axis + envelope.
usecase: Big-thing-wins service offset when clashing with a duct.
notfor: Non-duct obstructions — use OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE variants.
precondition: Active document; service + duct resolvable; both have BoundingBox; default 3D view.
postcondition: Zero or one ElementTransformUtils.MoveElement inside inner Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve service + duct (resolvers or two-element selection in order).
2) Read both BoundingBoxes; WireError if either missing.
3) Determine vertical relationship: if service centre Z ≤ duct centre Z → axis = -Z (offset below); else +Z (above). "auto" default.
4) step_ft = duct.Max.{axis} - duct.Min.{axis} (duct's extent on that axis) + envelope_mm/304.8.
5) Delegate TryDirectionalMoveOnService.ProbeAndMove(doc, service, axis, step_ft, threshold_ft, centreFallback=false).
6) Emit outcome + picked_axis + step_mm.

successprompts: Offset around duct by {step_mm} mm on {picked_axis}.

desc: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceByBoundingBoxEdgeCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE".
summary: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceByBoundingBoxEdgeCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE". Best-cardinal offset by BB edge length + envelope.
usecase: Best-fit cardinal offset when direction is not pre-committed.
notfor: Non-cardinal offsets — use Voxel routing.
precondition: Active document; one service resolvable with BoundingBox; default 3D view.
postcondition: Zero or one MoveElement inside inner Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve element. 2) Read BoundingBox; compute edge lengths (X, Y, Z).
3) CastSixDirections capped at max edge*2.
4) Build axis table: each direction's step = its corresponding edge length + envelope; clearance from probe.
5) If Resolver_PreferHorizontal=true, filter out ±Z first; else consider all.
6) Pick the axis with largest clearance >= threshold + step (room for the offset + envelope).
7) If found, delegate to TryDirectionalMoveOnService.ProbeAndMove with (axis, step, threshold, centreFallback=false).
8) If not, emit { applied=false, reason=\"no_axis_over_threshold\" }.

preprompts: Picking best cardinal offset by BB edge + envelope…
thinkingsteps: Reading BB edges and six-direction clearances…

desc: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceDownCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE_DOWN".
summary: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceDownCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE_DOWN". Step = service height + envelope.
usecase: Solver pushes service toward floor.
notfor: Fixed-step nudge — use MoveObjectDownOrCentreInSpace.
precondition: Active document; one service resolvable with BoundingBox; default 3D view.
postcondition: Zero or one MoveElement inside inner Transaction.
logictree: 1) Resolve element. 2) height from BB. 3) step = height + envelope/304.8. 4) Delegate to DirectionalMoveService with -Z, centreFallback=false. 5) Emit.

desc: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceUpCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE_UP".
summary: ClashSolverOffsetServiceTowardSpaceUpCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_TOWARD_SPACE_UP". Step = service height + envelope.
usecase: Solver lifts a service by its own height + clearance envelope.
notfor: Fixed-step nudges — use MoveObjectUpOrCentreInSpace.
precondition: Active document; one service resolvable with BoundingBox; default 3D view.
postcondition: One MoveElement inside the directional service's Transaction, or no-op.
logictree:
1) Resolve element (resolver or selection).
2) Read BoundingBox; height_ft = Max.Z - Min.Z; WireError if missing.
3) envelope_mm = Resolver_EnvelopeMm ?? 25.
4) step_ft = height_ft + envelope_mm/304.8.
5) Delegate to TryDirectionalMoveOnService.ProbeAndMove(doc, el, +Z, step_ft, threshold_ft, centreFallback=false).
6) Emit outcome + service_height_mm + envelope_mm.

preprompts: Offsetting service upward by height + envelope…

desc: ClashSolverOffsetServiceUnderAllBeamsInViewCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_UNDER_ALL_BEAMS_IN_VIEW".
summary: ClashSolverOffsetServiceUnderAllBeamsInViewCommand — PDF "OFFSET_SERVICE_UNDER_ALL_BEAMS_IN_VIEW". Bulk drop of services below the lowest beam soffit in the active view.
usecase: Coordination pre-pass to remove routine beam clashes.
notfor: Per-element targeted offsets — use OFFSET_SERVICE_AROUND_DUCT or MOVE_OBJECT_DOWN.
precondition: Active view with StructuralFraming elements visible.
postcondition: Zero or more ElementTransformUtils.MoveElement calls, each inside its own Transaction.
logictree:
1) Enumerate StructuralFraming in active view; WireError if none.
2) beam_soffit_z_ft = min over beams of bb.Min.Z (lowest underside in view).
3) Gather candidate services from Resolver_ElementIds (Bot) OR FilteredElementCollector scoped to active view for Pipes/Ducts/CableTrays (Human).
4) For each service: compute drop_ft = max(0, (service bb.Max.Z - beam_soffit_z_ft) + envelope_ft).
5) If drop_ft > 1e-6, delegate TryDirectionalMoveOnService.ProbeAndMove(doc, service, -Z, drop_ft, threshold_ft, centreFallback=false). Otherwise emit {already_below_beams}.
6) Aggregate and emit summary.

preprompts: Dropping services below all in-view beams…
thinkingsteps: Dropping each candidate service via DirectionalMoveService…

desc: ClashSolverStampErrorAndErrorTypeOnElementCommand — PDF FUNCTION "IF_ERROR_APPEND_WITH_ERROR".
summary: ClashSolverStampErrorAndErrorTypeOnElementCommand — PDF FUNCTION "IF_ERROR_APPEND_WITH_ERROR". Stamp the canonical `Error` + `Error Type` shared params on one element.
usecase: Manual escalation step from the clash-solver logic tree when no fix is possible.
notfor: Read-only queries — use the QA Manager dashboard.
precondition: Active document; target element resolvable; shared params bound to its category.
postcondition: Shared params written inside one Transaction; WireError on missing-binding / read-only.

2) Resolver_ErrorType must be one of StampClashFixReasonOnElement.Types; WireError otherwise.
3) Resolver_Message required, non-empty.
4) Open Transaction "ClashSolver::Stamp"; call StampClashFixReasonOnElement.Stamp(); commit.
5) On success: CentralWarningLogger.LogWarning + tracker.SetOutput(stamped=true).
6) On failure: stamp returns false → WireError already fired inside the service; tracker.SetOutput(stamped=false).

desc: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestNodeIn2dCommand — plan-view 2D connect-into-nearest.
summary: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestNodeIn2dCommand — plan-view 2D connect-into-nearest. Distance measured XY only; falls back to doc.Create.NewElbowFitting when ConnectTo refuses.
usecase: Plan-view routing where Z is fixed by the user.
notfor: 3D routing — use the 3D variant.
precondition: Active document; source element + connector resolvable; at least one candidate within 2D radius at the same Z.
postcondition: Either a direct Connector.ConnectTo or a doc.Create.NewElbowFitting inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve source connector (same rule as 3D).
2) Candidate pool restricted to |Δz| ≤ Resolver_ZToleranceMm; 2D distance ≤ Resolver_MaxSearchRadiusMm.
3) System-match gate.
4) Rank by 2D distance.
5) Transaction "ConnectorOps::ConnectNearest2d"; try source.ConnectTo(pick).
   If that leaves IsConnected=false, try doc.Create.NewElbowFitting(source, pick). Commit on success.
6) Emit result + used_fitting flag.

preprompts: Searching for nearest compatible connector on plan…
thinkingsteps: Restricting candidates to the same Z level…
Calling Connector.ConnectTo; falling back to NewElbowFitting if needed…

desc: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestNodeIn3dCommand — the solver's 3D connect-into-nearest.
summary: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestNodeIn3dCommand — the solver's 3D connect-into-nearest. Uses the system-match resolver as a gate and the canonical ConnectTo API for the actual join.
usecase: Wire up a specific unconnected end to the nearest compatible candidate.
notfor: Bulk connection — use it per end.
precondition: Active document; source element + connector resolvable; at least one candidate connector within radius.
postcondition: Exactly one Connector.ConnectTo call inside one Transaction; no other mutation.
logictree:
1) Resolve source: Resolver_ElementUniqueId required. If Resolver_ConnectorId given, use it; else pick the single unconnected connector (WireError if 0 or multiple).
2) Build candidate pool: every element with a ConnectorManager in the doc, every connector on those, filtered to same Domain + IsConnected=false + not on source element, within MaxSearchRadiusMm.
3) For each candidate, call MatchSystemByName.Evaluate. Drop Abort decisions. Keep Proceed directly; keep ProceedWithWarning only if Resolver_AllowCrossSystemWithWarning == true.
4) Rank by source→candidate Euclidean distance (feet). If empty, WireError "no_candidate".
5) Open Transaction "ConnectorOps::ConnectNearest3d"; source.ConnectTo(nearest); commit. WireRecoverable + RollBack on API throw.
6) Emit connected, target_element_id, target_connector_id, distance_mm, match_decision, reason.

successprompts: Connected to element {target_element_id} / connector {target_connector_id} at {distance_mm} mm.

desc: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestTerminalViaFlexCommand — route one flex segment between a main-run unconnected connector and the nearest terminal connector.
summary: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestTerminalViaFlexCommand — route one flex segment between a main-run unconnected connector and the nearest terminal connector.
usecase: Grille / diffuser / FCU link-up, field-style flex connection.
notfor: Rigid pipe runs — use the 3D or 2D connect commands.
precondition: Active document; source + terminal resolvable; same Domain; both connectors currently unconnected.
postcondition: Exactly one FlexDuct or FlexPipe created inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolver_SourceUniqueId + Resolver_TerminalUniqueId required.
2) Resolve source + terminal elements + pick one unconnected connector each (by Resolver_*ConnectorId or single-unconnected rule).
3) MatchSystemByName.Evaluate on the pair; abort if Abort.
4) Build 4-point waypoints: source.Origin, source.Origin + source.BasisZ * stub, terminal.Origin - terminal.BasisZ * stub, terminal.Origin.
5) Transaction "ConnectorOps::FlexTerminal"; per Domain, call FlexDuct.Create / FlexPipe.Create with waypoints and type defaults. Commit on success.
6) Emit flex_element_id, length_mm, waypoints_ft.

preprompts: Routing flex between source and nearest terminal…

desc: ConnectorOpsInsertFittingOnCurveAtStationCommand — two-step split-then-insert orchestration inside one Transaction.
summary: ConnectorOpsInsertFittingOnCurveAtStationCommand — two-step split-then-insert orchestration inside one Transaction.
usecase: Precise fitting placement (e.g. union mid-run).
notfor: Arbitrary-kind fittings beyond Elbow/Tee/Union/Transition.
precondition: Pipe resolvable; valid station; valid fitting kind.
postcondition: One fitting + two pipe segments committed or rolled back together.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ElementUniqueId OR single Pipe selection.
2) Resolver_DistanceFromEnd0Mm within bounds.
3) Parse Resolver_FittingKind.
4) Open Transaction 'ConnectorOps::SplitAndInsert'. Split via CurveSplitterService. Insert via FittingInserterService using left's end-1 + right's end-0 connectors (colinear for Union; for Elbow/Tee caller must supply second connector by convention).
5) On any failure roll back; WireError with message.
6) Emit fitting_id + left_id + right_id.

postcondition: Two pipes + union fitting inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ElementUniqueId required OR single Pipe selection.
2) Resolver_DistanceFromEnd0Mm required and within bounds.
3) Delegate to SplitCurveAtStation.SplitAtDistanceFromEnd0 (opens its own Transaction via SplitAtStation).
4) Emit left_id + right_id.

desc: Phase 7 Command: Creates a section view along a selected gridline.
summary: Phase 7 Command: Creates a section view along a selected gridline. User selects TWO inputs: 1. Gridline (from host or linked model) 2. Room (from host or linked model) Uses grid-room boundary intersection for section bounds. Calls CreateSectionFromGridline for the actual section creation. Uses FindClosestGridlineToPoint.FindClosestIntersectingGrid in auto mode. Uses FindCentroidOfRoom.GetCentroid for room centroid calculation. Called by SheetSetUpCommand during automated sheet setup. Related: CreateSectionForCorridorCommand for corridor sections. Scale: 1:50
usecase: User wants to create a section view showing MEP services along a specific grid line. User needs to see building cross-section at a particular grid location. User wants section through a room at a grid intersection.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. User must have access to select elements. At least one grid and one room must exist in the model (host or linked).
postcondition: A new ViewSection is created in the document, oriented along the selected gridline. Section bounds are clipped to the room boundary intersection. Section is NOT placed on a sheet (separate command).
logictree: check: currentSelection.hasGrid → useSelectedGridcheck: visibleGrids.count == 1 → autoSelectGridcheck: currentSelection.hasRoom → useSelectedRoomcheck: visibleRooms.count == 1 → autoSelectRoomcheck: visibleRooms.count > 1 → ask "Which room?"resolve: room.isCorridor → CreateSectionForCorridorCommand, else → this

desc: Creates a section view from user-specified start and end points or element endpoints.
summary: Creates a section view from user-specified start and end points or element endpoints. User picks two points (or selects a linear element) to define the section cut line. Section is created perpendicular to the line between points, looking from start towards end. Uses CreateSectionFromObject helper for the actual section creation. Related: CreateSectionFromGridlineCommand for grid-aligned sections.
usecase: User wants to create a section view through two arbitrary points. User wants to create a section view along a linear element (pipe, duct, wall). User needs a custom section not aligned to any grid.
notfor: Creating sections along gridlines (use CreateSectionFromGridlineCommand). Creating sections through corridors (use CreateSectionForCorridorCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open with an active view. User must be able to pick points or select elements in the view.
postcondition: A new ViewSection is created in the document, oriented perpendicular to the picked line. Section is NOT placed on a sheet (separate command).

preprompts: Creating a section view from your selected points...
thinkingsteps: Determining section depth and height from levels...

desc: Phase 7 Command: Creates a new sheet with user-specified parameters.
summary: Phase 7 Command: Creates a new sheet with user-specified parameters. Resolves titleblock from parameters (ID or name), auto-generates sheet number with "M-" prefix. Uses CreateSheet helper for the actual sheet creation. Uses ListSheets.GetNextAvailableNumber for auto-numbering. After creation, use CreateViewsCommand to populate with views.
usecase: User wants to create a new drawing sheet for MEP layouts. User needs a sheet with a specific number and name. AI orchestrator needs to create sheets as part of automated sheet setup.
notfor: Placing views on an existing sheet (use PlaceViewOnSheetCommand). Creating multiple sheets in batch (use SheetSetUpCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one titleblock family must be loaded in the project.
postcondition: A new ViewSheet is created in the document with the specified number and name. Titleblock is placed on the sheet. Sheet is ready for views to be placed.

config_inputs.name: titleblockId
config_inputs.description: Element ID of titleblock to use

config_inputs.name: titleblockName
config_inputs.description: Name of titleblock family to use

successprompts: Done! Sheet is ready for views.

desc: Command: Creates similar views for each primary level based on current view.
summary: Command: Creates similar views for each primary level based on current view. Extracts settings from the active view and replicates to all other levels. Uses ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevels for level discovery. Uses ListViews to check for existing views. Related: CreateViewsCommand for service-based view creation. Related: CreateSheetCommand for placing views on sheets. WORKFLOW: 1. Gets the current active view (must be a ViewPlan) 2. Extracts view settings: template, scope box, scale, crop region, detail level, etc. 3. Gets all primary levels using ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevels 4. For each level (except current), creates a new view with same settings 5. Names views consistently based on source view pattern 6. Reports success with list of created views
usecase: User has set up a view for one level and wants identical views on all floors. User needs to create consistent MEP plan views across all levels. User wants to propagate view setup from one floor to all others.
notfor: Custom UI required for preview/confirmation only. AI path is JSON-first and dialog-free.
precondition: Active document must be open with an active ViewPlan. At least two primary levels must exist (current + targets). ViewPlan must be a floor plan or ceiling plan.
postcondition: New views are created for each primary level (except the source level). All views match the source view's template, scope box, scale, and other settings. View names follow consistent pattern based on source view name.
sideeffects: Creates new ViewPlan elements in the document.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continuecheck: activeView.isViewPlan → continue, else → erroraction: extractViewSettings → template, scopeBox, scale, crop, etc.action: getPrimaryLevels → ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevelscheck: primaryLevels.count >= 2 → continue, else → erroraction: filterLevels → exclude source levelaction: createViewsForEachLevel → CreateViews helperaction: applySettings → match all propertiesresolve: success → showSuccess with created views

selection_inputs.name: SourceView
selection_inputs.description: Source view to copy settings from
selection_inputs.prompt: Active view is used as source

config_inputs.name: IncludeSourceLevel
config_inputs.description: Whether to skip or include the source level

config_inputs.name: NamePattern
config_inputs.description: Pattern for naming views, use {Level} for level name

Creating matching views for each floor...
thinkingsteps: Extracting view settings from source view...
Creating views for each target level...
Applying template, scope box, and scale settings...
successprompts: Created {ViewCount} views matching your source view settings.
Done! Views created for all levels.

desc: Creates a TextNote in the active view at user-provided XY coordinates (in mm).
summary: Creates a TextNote in the active view at user-provided XY coordinates (in mm). Optionally targets a named TextNoteType via ListText.GetTextNoteType; falls back to ListText.GetDefaultTextNoteType when no type is specified. Uses CommandExecutor.GetParameter for AI-resolved inputs.
usecase: User wants to place a text annotation on a plan or section view.
notfor: Creating keynotes or tags (use tagging commands). Not for sheet title blocks.
precondition: Active view must support text notes (not a schedule, legend, or 3D view).
postcondition: A new TextNote element exists in the active view at the specified location.

outputs.description: Created text note id, view, textType, and placement coordinates

preprompts: Placing a text note in the active view...
thinkingsteps: Converting mm coordinates to internal units...
successprompts: Text note placed in {view} at ({x}, {y}) mm
Done — text note created using type "{textType}"

desc: Phase 7 Command: Creates plan views based on detected MEP services, levels, and scope boxes.
summary: Phase 7 Command: Creates plan views based on detected MEP services, levels, and scope boxes. Detects active service types using ListServices, gets levels via ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevels. Creates floor or ceiling plans per service type using CreateViews helper. Scope boxes from ListScopeBoxes.GetAll are optionally assigned to views. Related: CreateSimilarViewsForLevelsCommand for replicating a single view. Related: CreateSheetCommand for sheet creation. Scale: 1:50 default.
usecase: User wants to create all MEP plan views for a project in one operation. AI orchestrator needs to batch-create views for specific services and levels. User wants views auto-assigned to scope boxes for consistent sheet layouts.
notfor: Creating a single view for one level (use CreateSimilarViewsForLevelsCommand). Creating sections (use CreateSectionFromGridlineCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one MEP service must be detected in the model. At least one primary level must exist.
postcondition: Plan views are created for each combination of service type, level, and scope box. Views are named as "{ServiceType} - {Level} - {ScopeBox}". Scope boxes are assigned to views when useScopeBoxes is true.

config_inputs.name: serviceTypes
config_inputs.description: JSON array of service type names to process

config_inputs.name: levelNames
config_inputs.description: JSON array of level names to process

config_inputs.name: scopeBoxNames
config_inputs.description: JSON array of scope box names to use

Setting up views for all services and levels...
thinkingsteps: Creating views for each service × level × scope box...
successprompts: Created {count} views across {services} services and {levels} levels.
Done! All MEP plan views are ready.

desc: Creates a new view template.
summary: Creates a new view template. When sourceViewName is set, duplicates settings from that view via CreateTemplate.FromView. Otherwise builds a coordinated MEP template using CreateTemplate.CreateMepStandard with templateName, serviceType, and scale.
precondition: Writable document; valid parameter combination (see remarks).
postcondition: New view template element exists when the helper returns a non-null view.

config_inputs.name: templateName
config_inputs.description: Name for the new view template

config_inputs.name: sourceViewName
config_inputs.description: Optional seed view (exact name) for FromView

preprompts: Choosing between duplicate-from-view and MEP standard template paths...
thinkingsteps: Read templateName, optional sourceViewName, optional serviceType, and scale.
If sourceViewName is set, resolve it with ListViews.FindByExactName and call CreateTemplate.FromView.
If no source view, require serviceType and call CreateTemplate.CreateMepStandard.
Validate the seed view is not a template before FromView.
Parse scale with recoverable fallback to 100 when invalid.
Run the chosen factory inside a manual transaction; roll back on null result.

desc: Debug command: finds Revit's canvas panel window handle and dimensions.
summary: Debug command: finds Revit's canvas panel window handle and dimensions. Enumerates all child windows of the main Revit window to identify the drawing canvas by size, visibility, and aspect ratio match against the active Autodesk.Revit.UI.UIView. Uses MEPBridge.Revit.AIChat.Services.Execution.CommandFeedback for output.
usecase: Developer needs to identify the canvas window handle for screen-to-model coordinate mapping.
notfor: Production workflows. Modifying the model. End-user features.
precondition: Active Revit document must be open with a valid active view.
postcondition: Debug log written to canvas_debug.txt. Results fed back via CommandFeedback.

outputs.description: Debug analysis results including candidate windows and aspect ratios

Looking for the drawing canvas window handle...

desc: Debug command for testing ceiling void height calculations.
summary: Debug command for testing ceiling void height calculations. Picks a point and displays all related measurements: room detection via DetectRoomFromServiceStartPoint, ceiling via DetectCeilingHeightFromRoom, slab via DetectSlabAboveHeight, void depth via CalculateVoidHeightFromCeilingAndSlab, centre-of-void via CalculateMiddleOfVoid, and 3D raycast via RayTrace3D.
usecase: User wants to verify void-height calculations at a specific point.
notfor: Production use. Use CalculateMiddleOfVoid directly for runtime logic.
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid plan or 3D view.
postcondition: Debug results are displayed via CommandFeedback. No model changes.

thinkingsteps: Calculating void depth and centre elevation...
successprompts: Void analysis complete — depth {VoidDepth}, centre at {CenterHeight}.
Debug results ready. Void is {VoidDepth} deep.

desc: Imports containment (cable tray) systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization.
summary: Imports containment (cable tray) systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization. Uses JsonModelLoader to parse the JSON DTO, then routes through MepSegmentCreator, PlaceElbowAtCorner, TakeoffPlacementService, PlaceTransitionBetweenSizes, and ImportBlocksFromLinkedDwg for element creation. Family downloads handled by LoadFamilyFromAdelphosCloud. Base point resolved via DesignSystemBasePointHelper. Related: DesignSmallPowerSystemCommand, DesignLightingSystemCommand.
usecase: User wants to import a containment (cable tray) layout from AutoCAD via MEPBridge JSON. User needs to place high-level or low-level containment routes with fittings and accessories.
notfor: Creating containment manually element-by-element (use Revit native tools). Importing piping or ductwork (use DesignDrainageSystemCommand or ventilation commands). Editing existing containment routes (use Revit editing tools).
precondition: Active Revit document must be open. MEPBridge JSON file must exist at the configured path. Cable tray family types must be loaded in the model. Level must exist near the base point Z.
postcondition: Cable tray segments, elbow fittings, tee fittings, transitions, and containment accessories are created in the model. A 3D section box is set around imported elements. A run log is written.

selection_inputs.name: BasePoint
selection_inputs.prompt: Pick Revit basepoint (target origin for import)

outputs.name: CurvesCreated
outputs.description: Number of MEP curve segments created

successprompts: Containment imported! Created {CurvesCreated} segments, {ElbowsPlaced} elbows, {BlocksPlaced} accessories.
Done! Cable tray layout placed with {CurvesCreated} segments and {BlocksPlaced} accessories.

desc: Design Drainage System Command: Imports drainage/sanitary systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization.
summary: Design Drainage System Command: Imports drainage/sanitary systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization. User selects TWO inputs: 1. JSON file path 2. Base point for placement. Uses silent Excel auto-mapping for blocks (no manual mapping UI). Creates pipes, fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and places notes for SVPs and fixtures.
usecase: User wants to design the drainage system from AutoCAD JSON export. User wants to import drainage pipework from coordinated model. User wants automated drainage routing from a coordinated JSON model. User says "design the drainage" or "create drainage system". User says "design sanitary" or "design soil" or "design waste".
notfor: Creating drainage from scratch without JSON input. Manual pipe routing (use standard Revit tools). Editing existing drainage systems.
precondition: Active document must be open. User must have MEPBridge JSON file. At least one level must exist in the model. Appropriate pipe types (Soil, PVC, Cast Iron) must be loaded in the model.
postcondition: MEP elements (pipes), fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and notes are created. Log file is written next to the JSON file with .drainage.log.txt extension. Neural Loading dialog shows completion summary.
logictree:
check: jsonPath.isValid → proceed
check: basePoint.isSelected → proceed
check: dto.Edges.count > 0 → proceed
resolve: excelMapper.exists → autoMap silently

selection_inputs.prompt: Pick Revit basepoint (target origin for import)

desc: Imports fire alarm systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization.
summary: Imports fire alarm systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization. Uses JsonModelLoader to parse the JSON DTO, then routes through MepSegmentCreator, PlaceElbowAtCorner, TakeoffPlacementService, PlaceTransitionBetweenSizes, and FamilyHostingResolver for intelligent device placement. Includes hardcoded fire alarm block-to-family mappings for smoke detectors, call points, beacons, etc. Family downloads handled by LoadFamilyFromAdelphosCloud with background prefetch. Also imports polylines and MText via ImportDetailLinesFromLinkedDwg and ImportTextFromLinkedDwg. Base point resolved via DesignSystemBasePointHelper. Related: DesignContainmentSystemCommand, DesignLightingSystemCommand.
usecase: User wants to import a fire alarm layout from AutoCAD via MEPBridge JSON. User needs to place smoke/heat detectors, manual call points, beacons, and sounders.
notfor: Creating fire alarm systems manually. Importing lighting or small power systems. Editing existing fire alarm device positions.
precondition: Active Revit document must be open. MEPBridge JSON file must exist. Cable tray types must be loaded. Level must exist near base point Z. For hosting resolution, ceiling elements or 2700mm fake ceiling height is used.
postcondition: MEP segments, fittings, fire alarm devices (detectors, call points, beacons, sounders), detail lines, and text notes are created. A placement report is written.

selection_inputs.description: Base point for fire alarm placement
selection_inputs.prompt: Pick Revit basepoint (target origin for import)

desc: Design Ventilation System Command: Imports MEP systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization.
summary: Design Ventilation System Command: Imports MEP systems from a MEPBridge JSON file with Neural Loading visualization. User selects TWO inputs: 1. JSON file path 2. Base point for placement. Uses silent Excel auto-mapping for blocks (no manual mapping UI). Creates ducts, pipes, cable trays, fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and family instances.
usecase: User wants to design the ventilation system from AutoCAD JSON export. User wants to import MEP ductwork and equipment from coordinated model. User wants automated ventilation routing from a coordinated JSON model. User says "design the ventilation" or "create ventilation system".
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. User must have MEPBridge JSON file. At least one level must exist in the model. Appropriate MEP types (duct, pipe, cable tray) must be loaded in the model.
postcondition: MEP elements (ducts, pipes, trays), fittings (elbows, tees, transitions), and blocks are created. Log file is written next to the JSON file with .ventilation.log.txt extension. Neural Loading dialog shows completion summary.
logictree:
check: jsonPath.isValid → proceed
check: basePoint.isSelected → proceed
check: dto.Edges.count > 0 → proceed
resolve: excelMapper.exists → autoMap silently
keywords: can you please design the ventilation
