selection_inputs.prompt: Pick Revit basepoint (target origin for import)

outputs.description: Number of MEP curve segments created

successprompts: Ventilation system created successfully! {DuctsCreated} segments, {ElbowsPlaced} elbows, {TeesPlaced} tees placed.
Done! Your ventilation system is ready with {DuctsCreated} MEP curves.
Ventilation design complete! Created {DuctsCreated} ducts with {ElbowsPlaced} fittings.

desc: DrainageAddBranchToSoilVentPipeCommand — split stack + create branch + insert tee inside one Transaction.
summary: DrainageAddBranchToSoilVentPipeCommand — split stack + create branch + insert tee inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Validate resolvers.
2) Transaction. Split stack at station.
3) Create new branch Pipe from split station along (dir_x,dir_y,dir_z) for length_mm.
4) Insert Tee at stack split facing connectors + branch start connector.
5) Commit on full success, rollback on any failure.

desc: Centres adjacent mover elements around a kept reference element by translating them along the kept element's direction axis.
summary: Centres adjacent mover elements around a kept reference element by translating them along the kept element's direction axis.
usecase: Re-centre a group of services on a kept plant item.
notfor: Height / slope alignment (use the Match* commands). Rotation.
precondition: Active document. 1 kept + ≥1 mover resolved. Kept element has a recoverable direction.
postcondition: Each mover translated along kept axis. Kept element untouched.
logictree: 1) Resolve kept + movers. 2) Compute kept centroid + direction. 3) Project mover centroid offsets onto kept axis, compute mean. 4) Transaction: translate each mover by −mean along kept axis. 5) Commit, log. 6) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

desc: Aligns the vertical centre (bbox mid-Z) of every moving element to the reference element's bbox centre, in a single Revit transaction.
summary: Aligns the vertical centre (bbox mid-Z) of every moving element to the reference element's bbox centre, in a single Revit transaction.
usecase: Align adjacent pipes / ducts / luminaires to a datum element's centre.
notfor: Top-face alignment (use ByTop). Underside (use ByUnderside). Horizontal moves.
precondition: Active document. Reference + at least one mover resolved. All elements expose a bounding box.
postcondition: Each mover translated by (0, 0, dz) feet to match reference centre. Audit entry written.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies reference + mover ids. 2) Validate both resolvers. 3) Open Transaction, compute ΔZ via CalculateElementAlignment, call ElementTransformUtils.MoveElement per mover. 4) Commit, log move summary. 5) Any failure → WireFault + rollback (Transaction dispose rolls back on uncommitted).

desc: Aligns top faces (bbox max.Z) of moving elements to the top face of the reference element.
summary: Aligns top faces (bbox max.Z) of moving elements to the top face of the reference element.
usecase: Tight-to-ceiling alignment — pipes, ducts, trays hugging a soffit.
notfor: Centre-line alignment (ByCentre). Underside alignment (ByUnderside).
precondition: Active document. 1 reference + ≥1 mover resolved. Bounding boxes available.
postcondition: Each mover translated by (0, 0, dz) feet. Audit entry written.
logictree: 1) Resolve reference + movers. 2) Validate bboxes. 3) Transaction: ΔZ = refTop − moverTop, MoveElement. 4) Commit, log. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

desc: Aligns undersides (bbox min.Z) of moving elements to the reference element's underside.
summary: Aligns undersides (bbox min.Z) of moving elements to the reference element's underside.
usecase: Preserve headroom below a service run — match undersides.
notfor: Top-face alignment (ByTop). Centre alignment (ByCentre).
precondition: Active document. 1 reference + ≥1 mover resolved. Bounding boxes available.
postcondition: Each mover translated by (0, 0, dz) feet. Audit entry written.
logictree: 1) Resolve reference + movers. 2) Validate bboxes. 3) Transaction: ΔZ = refMinZ − moverMinZ, MoveElement. 4) Commit, log. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

desc: Rotates each moving element about its start point so its direction vector matches the reference element's direction.
summary: Rotates each moving element about its start point so its direction vector matches the reference element's direction. Planar cases rotate about Z (safer for hosted families).
usecase: Align a pipe / duct / tray to match a reference direction.
notfor: Intersection-point-based slope alignment (use MatchSlopeIntersection). Height-only alignment.
precondition: Active document. 1 reference + ≥1 mover resolved. Both have recoverable directions.
postcondition: Each mover rotated in place so |dir − refDir| ≈ 0.
logictree: 1) Resolve reference + movers. 2) Extract reference direction. 3) Transaction: per mover compute rotation about Z (or full 3D if Z component ≠ 0) and call RotateElement. 4) Commit, log. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

desc: Rotates each moving element about the closest-approach point of its extended axis and the reference's extended axis, so the two axes end up intersecting with the reference direction.
summary: Rotates each moving element about the closest-approach point of its extended axis and the reference's extended axis, so the two axes end up intersecting with the reference direction.
usecase: Converging pipe / duct runs whose axes must meet at a specific apex.
notfor: Simple parallel slope matching (use MatchSlopeFromReferenceCommand).
precondition: Active document. 1 reference + ≥1 mover resolved. Both have directional geometry.
postcondition: Each mover rotated so its axis meets the reference axis at a common apex.
logictree: 1) Resolve reference + movers. 2) Extract reference direction + start. 3) Transaction: per mover compute closest-approach point of axes, compute ΔZ-rotation, RotateElement about axis Z through that apex. 4) Commit, log. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

desc: Command: Exports all room boundaries to JSON files for AutoCAD import.
summary: Command: Exports all room boundaries to JSON files for AutoCAD import. Creates one JSON file per level in the same folder as the Revit model. WORKFLOW: 1. User picks a base point (export origin) 2. Command retrieves all rooms from host + linked models 3. Creates ZoneDataStorage for each room with boundary coordinates 4. Uses ContextZones to filter and format export data 5. Exports one JSON file per level to model folder
usecase: User wants to export room boundaries for use in AutoCAD. User needs zone layouts for 2D coordination drawings. User wants to transfer spatial data between Revit and AutoCAD.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open and saved (for export path). At least one room must exist in the model or linked models.
postcondition: JSON files created in the Revit model folder, one per level. Files named: Zones_{LevelName}.json Coordinates are in mm, relative to the selected base point.
sideeffects: Creates JSON files in the model folder. Does not modify any Revit elements.
logictree: check: document.isSaved → continue, else → error (need save location)action: promptUserToPickBasePoint → getBasePointaction: getAllRoomsIncludingLinks → SearchInsideCurrentModel.GetAllRoomsIncludingLinkscheck: rooms.count > 0 → continue, else → error (no rooms found)loop: foreach room → create ZoneDataStorageaction: groupByLevel → ContextZones.CreateExportModelsForAllLevelsloop: foreach level → saveJsonFileresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

desc: Command: Extends pipes from all connectors on selected fitting(s)/object(s).
summary: Command: Extends pipes from all connectors on selected fitting(s)/object(s). Uses existing selection when elements are already selected (e.g. by MCP select_by_ids); otherwise prompts to pick one. Uses ExtendAllConnectors for the core extension logic. Called by ExtendAllConnectorsTool (MCP) or ribbon button. Related: ExtendFromConnector for single-connector extension. WORKFLOW: 1. If elements are selected, use them; else prompt user to pick one 2. For each selected element: get unconnected pipe connectors, create pipe extensions (default 100 mm) 3. Pipes match system type and connector size and are connected to the fitting
usecase: Quick pipe stub creation from fittings. Batch extend multiple fittings (pre-select then run). MCP: select_by_ids then extend_all_connectors for non-interactive execution.
notfor: When selection is empty and run from MCP, command will prompt for pick — pre-select via select_by_ids first.
precondition: Active document must be open. Selected element(s) must have pipe connectors (FamilyInstance with MEPModel or MEPCurve).
postcondition: Pipes created from all unconnected connectors on each selected element. Each pipe uses ExtensionLength (default 100 mm). Pipes are connected to the fitting.
sideeffects: Creates new pipe elements. Connects pipes to the selected fitting.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectElement → getElementcheck: elementHasConnectors → continue, else → erroraction: getUnconnectedPipeConnectors → connectorListaction: createPipeFromEachConnector → pipeListaction: connectPipesToFitting → connectresolve: success → complete, else → showError

desc: Declares one concrete feature of a command, tool or app surface.
summary: Declares one concrete feature of a command, tool or app surface. Multiple FeatureAttribute instances are allowed per class; they're emitted in declaration order to features[] in the registry JSON, which the website renders as a grid of labelled tiles on the page. USAGE: [Feature("Workshare-aware", "Works on local + central models; respects worksets.")] [Feature("Audited transactions", "Every write wrapped as a Revit transaction with one-click undo.")] [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)] public class ExtendAllConnectorsCommand : ExternalCommand { ... } GROUPING (optional): For richer, grouped feature lists (e.g. QA Manager's drawing / schedule / spec / model groups) set Group. The website will render one subsection per distinct group name.

desc: Command: Manual two-pick extend and connect for MEP elements.
summary: Command: Manual two-pick extend and connect for MEP elements. User explicitly selects: 1. Source element (pipe, fitting, or component) — click near connector to extend 2. Target element (pipe, fitting, or component) — click near connector to connect to Extends source by LINE INTERSECTION — finds where the two lines would meet if extended. Works with pipes, fittings, and equipment with connectors. Uses ExtendMEPCurve for pipe extension logic. Uses GetMEPCurve for pipe geometry queries. Uses HealConnectors for post-connection cleanup. Related: ExtendAndConnectPipesCommand for automatic (non-manual) variant.
usecase: Connecting pipe to fitting connector. Connecting pipe to equipment connector. Extending two pipes to meet at intersection. Manual control over which connectors to use.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. Both selected elements must have valid connectors. The connector directions must intersect (not parallel).
postcondition: Source element is extended to meet the target element's line. An elbow fitting connects the two elements.

selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET element - click near the connector to connect to

desc: Command: Extends a selected pipe endpoint to find and connect to a nearby pipe of the same system.
summary: Command: Extends a selected pipe endpoint to find and connect to a nearby pipe of the same system. Uses ExtendAndConnectPipes for the core search-extend-connect logic. Uses GetMEPCurve for pipe geometry queries. Uses ExtendMEPCurve and FilletMEPCurve for extension. Related: ExtendAndConnectManualCommand for manual two-pick variant. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects a pipe endpoint to extend 2. System searches for nearby pipes of the same piping system type 3. Finds the closest unconnected endpoint within search radius 4. Extends source pipe to match X/Y center of target 5. Extends source pipe to match Z elevation of target 6. Creates elbow fitting to connect the pipes
usecase: Connecting branch drains to main horizontal runs. Connecting horizontal pipes to vertical risers. Rapid connection during preliminary pipe layout. Auto-connecting pipes during MEP routing.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one other pipe of the same system must exist within search radius with an unconnected endpoint.
postcondition: Source pipe is extended to meet the target pipe. An elbow fitting connects the two pipes.
sideeffects: Modifies source pipe geometry (extends endpoint). Creates a new FamilyInstance (elbow fitting).
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectPipe → getSourcePipecheck: pipeSelected → continue, else → erroraction: getSelectionPoint → determineEndpointaction: executeExtendAndConnect → processConnectionresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

Suppresses "not enough room for fittings" warnings via MEPWarningSwallower.

desc: Command: Extends a selected pipe downwards by radius × 5.
summary: Command: Extends a selected pipe downwards by radius × 5. Uses ExtendPipeDown for the core extension logic. Related: ExtendFromConnectorCommand for connector-based extension. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects a pipe 2. Command gets pipe properties (system, size, routing type) 3. Delegates to ExtendPipeDown.Extend to create extension 4. Reports success/failure via CommandFeedback
usecase: User needs to extend a drainage pipe downwards. User wants to quickly add a below-slab extension. User is extending vertical stacks through multiple floors.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe must exist in the model. User must select a valid pipe element.
postcondition: A new pipe is created extending downwards from the bottom of the selected pipe. Extension length = pipe radius × 5. New pipe matches system, size and family routing types.
sideeffects: Creates a new pipe element in the model.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectPipe → getSelectedPipecheck: selectedElement.isPipe → continue, else → erroraction: getPipeProperties → system, size, routingaction: calculateExtensionLength → radius × 5action: createExtensionPipe → addToModelresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

selection_inputs.prompt: Select a pipe to extend downwards

config_inputs.description: Extension length as multiplier of radius

desc: Command: Extends a selected pipe upwards to the next primary level.
summary: Command: Extends a selected pipe upwards to the next primary level. INCLUDES LEVELS FROM LINKED MODELS (architectural links, etc.) WORKFLOW: 1. User selects a pipe endpoint 2. Command uses ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevels() to find level structure (host + linked models) 3. Determines current pipe position and next level above 4. Creates extension pipe to next primary level + 150mm offset 5. Matches all properties from source pipe 6. Optionally creates bend fitting at connection
usecase: User needs to extend an SVP or drainage riser to the next floor. User wants to grow vertical stacks through floor plates. User is extending vent pipes through ceiling/floor assemblies.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe must exist in the model. At least two primary levels must exist (current and target). User must select a valid pipe element.
postcondition: A new pipe is created extending from the selected pipe to the next primary level. Extension terminates at level elevation + 150mm offset. New pipe matches system, size and family routing types. Bend fitting created at connection point (optional).
sideeffects: Creates a new pipe element in the model. May create a bend fitting element.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectPipe → getSelectedPipecheck: selectedElement.isPipe → continue, else → erroraction: getPrimaryLevels → ListLevels.GetPrimaryLevelscheck: primaryLevels.count >= 2 → continue, else → error (need at least current + target)action: findCurrentLevel → LevelResolver.GetNearestLevelaction: findNextLevelAbove → compare elevationscheck: nextLevel.exists → continue, else → error (already at top)action: calculateExtensionLength → targetLevel.elevation + offset - currentZaction: createExtensionPipe → ExtendPipeToNextLevel.Extendresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: Pipe
selection_inputs.description: Pipe to extend to next level
selection_inputs.prompt: Select a pipe to extend to the next level

config_inputs.name: LevelOffset
config_inputs.description: Offset above level elevation in mm

config_inputs.description: Whether to create bend fitting at connection

desc: Filters the current selection, keeping only elements matching the filter criteria.
summary: Filters the current selection, keeping only elements matching the filter criteria. Supports keep/remove by category, family, type, and deep parameter search. Uses SearchParameterValue for deep parameter matching. Uses CommandExecutor to read filter parameters from Claude. Follow up with SelectByCategoryCommand or SelectByFamilyNameCommand for initial selection. Related: SelectAllInstancesCommand for model-wide family selection.
usecase: User selected all lights, now wants to keep only emergency lights (keepParameter="emergency"). User selected all electrical fixtures, now wants to remove exit signs (removeFamily="exit"). User wants to narrow selection by type name (keepType="Type A").
notfor: Initial element selection (use SelectByCategoryCommand, SelectByFamilyNameCommand first). Selecting by parameter search from scratch (use SelectByParameterCommand).
precondition: Elements must be selected in Revit. Use SelectByCategoryCommand or SelectByFamilyNameCommand first.
postcondition: Revit selection is narrowed to only elements passing the filter. Returns JSON with originalCount, filteredCount, removed count, and breakdown.
logictree: check: currentSelection.count == 0 → error with suggestionapply: keepCategory, keepFamily, keepType, keepParameter → AND logic (all must pass)apply: removeFamily, removeCategory → exclude matchingcheck: invert == "true" → swap kept/removed

desc: HeatingAddRadiatorDropsByValveSetCommand — create flow + return drops from a radiator and insert a valve set per leg.
summary: HeatingAddRadiatorDropsByValveSetCommand — create flow + return drops from a radiator and insert a valve set per leg.
logictree:
1) Resolve radiator (FamilyInstance).
2) Transaction. For each flow + return connector on the radiator:
   create Pipe from connector.Origin to Resolver_*TargetXyzFt.
3) Delegate valve-set insert to PipeworkAddValveSetToPipeByDefinitionCommand on each drop.
4) Emit pipe + valve ids.

desc: HeatingSizeRadiatorsAltitudeDeltaTCorrectionForSpaceCommand — altitude-corrected sizing.
summary: HeatingSizeRadiatorsAltitudeDeltaTCorrectionForSpaceCommand — altitude-corrected sizing.
logictree:
1) altitude_correction = max(0.5, 1 - 0.05 * altitude_m / 1000).
2) Delegate to HeatingSizeRadiatorsFromCalculatedLossForSpaceCommand with AltitudeCorrection = factor.
3) Tag tracker.

desc: HeatingSizeRadiatorsFromCalculatedLossForSpaceCommand — select a radiator via EN 442 from the project schedule.

notfor: Altitude-corrected variants — use the dedicated sibling commands.

postcondition: SelectedRadiatorType parameter set on the Room.
logictree:
1) Resolver_RoomId required; load Room + HeatLossWatts param.
2) Parse Resolver_ScheduleJson as an array of ScheduleEntry.
3) Call CalculateRadiatorSizeToEn442.Size(inputs).
4) On success: write SelectedRadiatorType on Room inside one Transaction.
5) Emit chosen + reason.

desc: Imports a full MEP model from JSON into the active Revit document.
summary: Imports a full MEP model from JSON into the active Revit document. Creates pipes, ducts, cable trays with correct sizes and systems, then places fittings (elbows, tees, transitions) and family blocks. Uses JsonModelLoader for JSON parsing. Uses TypeResolver for Revit type resolution. Uses MepSegmentCreator for MEP curve creation. Uses PlaceElbowAtCorner, TakeoffPlacementService, PlaceTransitionBetweenSizes for fitting placement. Uses ImportBlocksFromLinkedDwg for family block placement. Uses GetCurrentViewLevel and LevelResolver for level resolution. Related: ExportJSON for exporting, ImportRadCommand for radiators.
usecase: User wants to import a full MEP layout from a JSON design tool export. User wants to recreate MEP routing from a 2D/3D design into Revit. User needs to import pipes, ducts, trays, and equipment from JSON.
notfor: Importing only radiators (use ImportRadCommand). Importing IFC, DWG, or native Revit formats. Exporting from Revit (use ExportJSON).
precondition: Active document must be open. A valid JSON export file must exist. Required pipe/duct/tray types must be loaded in the model. At least one level must exist in the document.
postcondition: MEP curves, fittings, and family blocks are created in the document. A companion .import.log.txt file is written alongside the JSON file. Smart mapping and family export files are created for debugging.

successprompts: Import complete — {curvesCreated} curves, {elbows} elbows, {familiesPlaced} families placed.
MEP import finished successfully with {totalItems} elements.

Related: ImportRoutingPreferences for full routing-preference import including rule configuration.

Uses JsonModelLoader.Load to parse the JSON model. Uses GetRadiatorsFromScheduleCatalogue.LoadFromExcel to read the Excel schedule. Uses GetCurrentViewLevel.Get and LevelResolver.GetNearestLevel for level resolution. Uses UnitsHelper.RelativeMmToFeet_XY_UseBaseZ for coordinate conversion. Called by the ribbon or AI chat via MEPBridge.Revit.AIChat.Services.Execution.CommandExecutor. Related: ImportCommand for full MEP import.

notfor: Importing full MEP layouts with pipes, ducts, trays (use ImportCommand). Manually placing individual radiators (use Revit's family placement). Editing existing radiator parameters (use parameter editing commands).

postcondition: Radiator family instances are placed at JSON coordinates with types from the Excel schedule. Match results are exported to a rad-matches.json file alongside the source JSON. Schedule input is exported to rad-schedule-input.json for comparison.

usecase: User wants to copy a drainage system setup from a template model. User needs to bring in pipe types and fittings from another project. User wants to standardize system configurations across projects.

desc: Launches the standalone Document Controller WPF desktop app from a Revit ribbon button.
summary: Launches the standalone Document Controller WPF desktop app from a Revit ribbon button. The Document Controller is NOT hosted inside Revit — it runs as its own process (DocumentController.WPF.exe) and shares the Supabase backend. The command resolves the exe path from an environment variable override, then the published add-in folder, then known development build-output locations. If an instance is already running, it is brought to the foreground instead of launching a duplicate. Uses ErrorPanel for the three-wire error model and CommandFeedback for user notifications.
usecase: User clicks the "Doc Controller" ribbon button to open the standalone document management desktop app while continuing to work in Revit.
notfor: Hosting the Document Controller inside Revit (it is intentionally a separate process so it can run without Revit and share the web/desktop chat core). Launching from headless automation — requires a Windows desktop session.
precondition: DocumentController.WPF.exe must have been built at least once (dotnet build DocumentController/DocumentController.WPF/DocumentController.WPF.csproj) OR be deployed alongside the MEPBridge add-in in the ApplicationPlugins folder.
postcondition: A DocumentController.WPF window is visible on the desktop. If a prior instance existed, that window is brought to the foreground. If the exe cannot be found, a recoverable error is raised listing every probed path for the user.

preprompts: Launching the Document Controller desktop app...
thinkingsteps: Looking for DocumentController.WPF.exe in known locations...
successprompts: Document Controller was already running — brought to foreground.

desc: LayoutApplyPendingPlacementsToRevitCommand — commit a staged placement batch to the Revit model.
summary: LayoutApplyPendingPlacementsToRevitCommand — commit a staged placement batch to the Revit model.
usecase: Commit a plantroom solve result.
notfor: Dry-run / preview — use LayoutPreviewPlantroomSolveWithPreset.
precondition: Resolver_ChangeId resolvable in staging DB.
postcondition: FamilyInstances created inside one Transaction; change transitioned to 'applied'.
logictree:
1) Resolver_ChangeId required.
2) Load pending_placements for the change.
3) For each placement: resolve FamilySymbol (unique id first, then name fallback).
4) Open Transaction. Activate symbol if not active. Call NewFamilyInstance. Record per-placement outcome.
5) Commit; transition change to 'applied'. On no successes, roll back + mark 'rejected'.

desc: LayoutSolvePlantroomEquipmentWithPresetCommand — run solver + stage placements into pending_placements.
summary: LayoutSolvePlantroomEquipmentWithPresetCommand — run solver + stage placements into pending_placements.
usecase: Plantroom layout generation.
notfor: Applying to Revit — that's LayoutApplyPendingPlacementsToRevitCommand.
precondition: Resolver_PresetJson + Resolver_EquipmentJson + room dims required.
postcondition: Zero or more pending_placements rows + one solver_runs row.
logictree:
1) Validate resolvers.
2) Build CpSatRoom from room dims.
3) Parse equipment JSON.
4) CpSatPlantroomLayoutSolver.Solve (records solver_runs).
5) For each placement, PendingPlacementsRepository.Enqueue under a new change.
6) Emit run_id + placements_staged + diagnostics.

desc: Command: Matches pipe elevation, extends, and connects with a fitting.
summary: Command: Matches pipe elevation, extends, and connects with a fitting. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects source pipe (this pipe will be moved and extended) 2. User selects target pipe (elevation reference) 3. Source pipe is moved to match target elevation 4. Source pipe is extended to meet target 5. Elbow fitting is placed at junction
usecase: Connecting branch drains to main drains. Joining pipes at different elevations with proper fitting. Quick pipe connections with automatic elevation matching.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist. User must select two valid pipe elements.
postcondition: Source pipe endpoint matches target pipe endpoint elevation. Source pipe extended to meet target. Elbow fitting placed at connection point.
sideeffects: Moves the source pipe element vertically. Extends the source pipe geometry. Creates a fitting element.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectSourcePipe → getSourcePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: matchElevation → moveSourceaction: extendToMeet → extendSourceaction: placeFitting → createElbowresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: SourcePipe
selection_inputs.description: Pipe to move and extend (will be modified)
selection_inputs.prompt: Select SOURCE pipe (this pipe will be moved and extended)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.description: Pipe to connect to (stays in place)
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (source will connect to this)

preprompts: Matching pipe elevation and connecting with fitting...
Preparing to match height and place elbow...
successprompts: Pipes connected with fitting — elevation matched by {ElevationDelta}.
Height matched and elbow fitting placed successfully.

desc: Command: Matches the elevation (Z height) of one pipe's endpoint to another.
summary: Command: Matches the elevation (Z height) of one pipe's endpoint to another. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects source pipe (this pipe will be moved) 2. User selects target pipe (elevation reference) 3. Source pipe is moved vertically so endpoints align in elevation
usecase: Aligning drain pipes for proper connections. Matching branch drain elevation to main drain. Leveling pipe endpoints for fitting placement.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist in the model. User must select two valid pipe elements.
postcondition: Source pipe endpoint matches target pipe endpoint elevation. Source pipe is moved vertically by the elevation difference.
sideeffects: Moves the source pipe element vertically.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectSourcePipe → getSourcePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: calculateElevationDifference → deltaaction: moveSourcePipe → updateModelresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: SourcePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select SOURCE pipe (this pipe will be moved)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.description: Pipe to match elevation to (stays in place)
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (source will match this elevation)

thinkingsteps: Moving source pipe to target elevation...
successprompts: Pipe height matched — moved {ElevationDelta} to align.
Elevation aligned successfully between the two pipes.

desc: Command: Matches pipe centerlines by shifting the second pipe in X/Y.
summary: Command: Matches pipe centerlines by shifting the second pipe in X/Y. Delegates alignment logic to MatchPipeCenters. Uses GetMEPCurve indirectly via the logic class. Related: MEPSystemFilletCommand for full fillet, MEPSystemAutoFilletCommand for auto-detect elbow/tee. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects reference pipe (stays in place) 2. User selects target pipe (will be moved) 3. Target pipe is shifted in X/Y to align with reference pipe centerline 4. Pipes can now be connected with a fitting
usecase: Aligning branch pipes to main pipes before connecting. Fixing misaligned pipes that can't accept fittings. Preparing pipes for automatic connection with FilletMEPCurve.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist in the model.
postcondition: Target pipe centerline aligns with reference pipe extension. Pipes are now in position to be connected with an elbow.
sideeffects: Moves the target pipe element horizontally.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectReferencePipe → getReferencePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: calculateXYOffset → offsetaction: moveTargetPipe → shiftresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: ReferencePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select REFERENCE pipe (this pipe stays in place)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (this pipe will be shifted)

thinkingsteps: Calculating X/Y offset to align centerlines...
successprompts: Pipes aligned. Shifted by X={XOffset}, Y={YOffset}.
Centerlines matched — pipes ready for fitting.

desc: Command: Auto-detects and creates appropriate fitting (elbow or tee).
summary: Command: Auto-detects and creates appropriate fitting (elbow or tee). Delegates to MEPSystemAutoFillet which internally uses MEPSystemFillet for elbows and MEPSystemTee for tees. Related: MEPSystemFilletCommand for forced elbow, MatchPipeCentersCommand for alignment only. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects reference pipe 2. User selects target pipe 3. Command checks if reference selection is within 25 mm of unconnected endpoint 4. If within 25 mm: creates elbow fitting (aligns and connects) 5. If beyond 25 mm: creates tee fitting (breaks pipe and inserts tee)
usecase: Smart pipe connections without manual fitting selection. Creating either endpoint or mid-pipe connections. General-purpose pipe connection tool.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist.
postcondition: Appropriate fitting created based on selection proximity. Pipes are connected.
sideeffects: May move target pipe. May break reference pipe (for tee). Creates fitting element.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectReferencePipe → getReferencePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → errordecision: distanceToEndpoint ≤ 25mm → useElbow, else → useTeeaction: createFitting → connectresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: ReferencePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select REFERENCE pipe (click near endpoint for elbow, mid-pipe for tee)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe to connect

preprompts: Choosing elbow or tee based on selection...
thinkingsteps: Checking distance to nearest unconnected endpoint...
successprompts: Connected with {FittingType} — selection was {DistanceToEndpoint} from endpoint.

desc: Command: Forces a fillet connection between two pipes.
summary: Command: Forces a fillet connection between two pipes. Uses MEPSystemFillet for the core alignment and fitting logic. Calls MatchPipeCenters for X/Y alignment, ChangeElevationOfObject for Z alignment, and FilletMEPCurve / GetMEPCurve for fitting placement. Related: MEPSystemTeeCommand for mid-pipe tee connections. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects reference pipe (stays in place) 2. User selects target pipe (will be moved to connect) 3. Target pipe centers are matched to reference (X/Y) 4. Target pipe elevation is matched to reference (Z) 5. Elbow fitting is placed at connection
usecase: Forcing connections between misaligned pipes. Quick pipe connections with automatic alignment. Connecting branch pipes to main runs.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist in the model.
postcondition: Target pipe is aligned to reference pipe in X, Y, and Z. Elbow fitting connects the two pipes.
sideeffects: Moves the target pipe element. Creates a fitting element.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectReferencePipe → getReferencePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: matchCenters → alignXYaction: matchElevation → alignZaction: createFitting → connectresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: ReferencePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select REFERENCE pipe (stays in place)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.description: Target pipe (will be moved and connected)
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (will be moved to connect)

desc: Command: Creates a tee fitting connection between pipes.
summary: Command: Creates a tee fitting connection between pipes. Delegates to MEPSystemTee for the core break-and-insert logic. Uses GetMEPCurve for pipe geometry extraction. Related: MEPSystemFilletCommand for endpoint fillet connections. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects reference pipe (where tee will be inserted) 2. User selects target pipe (branch to connect) 3. Reference pipe is broken at selection point 4. Tee fitting is inserted 5. Target pipe connects to tee branch
usecase: Creating branch connections mid-pipe. Adding drainage branches to main runs. T-junction connections.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist.
postcondition: Tee fitting inserted at selection point. Target pipe connected to tee branch.
sideeffects: Breaks the reference pipe. Moves target pipe. Creates tee fitting.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectReferencePipe → getReferencePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: breakReferenceAtPoint → createBreakaction: insertTeeFitting → createTeeaction: connectTargetToBranch → connectresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: ReferencePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select REFERENCE pipe (where tee will be inserted)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (branch to connect)

desc: NetworkBreakAndOffset45DownOnSelectionCommand — break the selected pipe(s) and insert a 45° downward ramp that re-joins below.
summary: NetworkBreakAndOffset45DownOnSelectionCommand — break the selected pipe(s) and insert a 45° downward ramp that re-joins below.
usecase: User needs to dodge a clash by stepping a run down-and-under.
notfor: Rectangular-duct offsets (need a separate fitting-lookup strategy).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe selected in the UI.
postcondition: Selected pipes are broken and re-joined through a 45° down-ramp of the requested ΔZ.
logictree: 1) Pull selection → require ≥1 Pipe. 2) Resolve ΔZ (resolver, default 150 mm). 3) For each pipe: compute plan via GetBreakAndOffsetGeometry. 4) Open Transaction "Break & Offset 45° Down". 5) Apply each plan via ApplyBreakAndOffsetToPipe. 6) Commit, report counts. 7) Failure → ErrorWire.WireFault + CentralWarningLogger.LogError.

desc: NetworkBreakAndOffset45UpOnSelectionCommand — break the selected pipe(s) and insert a 45° upward ramp that re-joins above.
summary: NetworkBreakAndOffset45UpOnSelectionCommand — break the selected pipe(s) and insert a 45° upward ramp that re-joins above.
usecase: User needs to clear an obstacle overhead by stepping a run up-and-over.
notfor: Rectangular-duct offsets (separate strategy).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe selected.
postcondition: Selected pipes are broken and re-joined through a 45° up-ramp of the requested ΔZ.
logictree: 1) Selection → ≥1 Pipe. 2) Resolve ΔZ + flat-top. 3) Plan via geometry service. 4) Transaction "Break & Offset 45° Up". 5) Apply via applier service. 6) Commit, report. 7) Failures → ErrorWire.WireFault + CentralWarningLogger.

desc: NetworkBreakAndOffset90DownOnSelectionCommand — 90° vertical drop on selection.
summary: NetworkBreakAndOffset90DownOnSelectionCommand — 90° vertical drop on selection.
usecase: Fitting a run through a low-clearance zone that needs a hard step-down.
notfor: Rectangular duct.
precondition: Active document. ≥1 pipe in selection.
postcondition: Pipes broken and re-routed through a 90° down dog-leg.
logictree: 1) Selection → ≥1 Pipe. 2) Resolve ΔZ + flat-top. 3) Plan @ 90° Down. 4) Transaction "Break & Offset 90° Down". 5) Apply. 6) Commit, report.

desc: NetworkBreakAndOffset90UpOnSelectionCommand — 90° vertical step-up on selection.
summary: NetworkBreakAndOffset90UpOnSelectionCommand — 90° vertical step-up on selection.
usecase: Lifting a run over a beam by a fixed amount via hard elbows.
notfor: Rectangular duct.

desc: NetworkBreakAndRiseGroupByConnectedComponentCommand — lifts every pipe on the seed's connected component that runs parallel to the seed pipe, through the requested ramp profile.
summary: NetworkBreakAndRiseGroupByConnectedComponentCommand — lifts every pipe on the seed's connected component that runs parallel to the seed pipe, through the requested ramp profile.
usecase: User wants to re-route an entire branch of a run over a shared obstacle.
notfor: Mixed-system runs (graph spans systems — flagged and skipped per pipe).
precondition: Active document. A seed pipe selected (or set via Resolver_SeedId).
postcondition: All parallel pipes in the seed's connected component are broken and re-routed through the chosen ramp profile in one undo step.
logictree: 1) Resolve seed pipe. 2) Build component via GetConnectedComponentGraphOfNetwork. 3) Enumerate MEPCurve elements; filter by bearing (± tolerance). 4) Transaction "Break & Rise (Component)". 5) For each edge: plan + apply. 6) Commit, count. 7) Failures → WireFault.

successprompts: Lifted {count} pipes across the connected component.

desc: PipeworkAddIsolationValvesAtEndRunsCommand — install isolation fittings at every end-run connector in a network component.
summary: PipeworkAddIsolationValvesAtEndRunsCommand — install isolation fittings at every end-run connector in a network component.
usecase: Bulk isolation.
notfor: Per-pair insertion — use the per-pair ConnectorOps command.
precondition: Seed element ids in a valid MEP component.
postcondition: 0..N fittings committed in one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve seeds (resolver or active selection).
2) Build graph → find end-run edges.
3) For each end-run connector, insert the chosen FittingKind if not already connected.
4) Commit / rollback single Transaction.
5) Emit counts + fitting ids.
keywords: add isolation valves at end runs

preprompts: Adding isolation valves at every end-run connector…

desc: PipeworkAddValveSetToPipeByDefinitionCommand — insert an ordered valve set on a pipe from a JSON definition.
summary: PipeworkAddValveSetToPipeByDefinitionCommand — insert an ordered valve set on a pipe from a JSON definition.
usecase: Adding an isolation assembly to a distribution pipe.
notfor: Single fitting insert — use ConnectorOpsInsertFittingOnCurveAtStationCommand.
precondition: Pipe resolvable; station within pipe length; valve-set JSON parses.
postcondition: N fittings + (N+1) pipe segments inside one Transaction, or rollback.
logictree:
1) Resolver_PipeUniqueId + Resolver_StationMm + Resolver_ValveSetJson required.
2) Parse JSON as [{ "symbol_unique_id": "...", "offset_mm": 50, "kind": "Union|Elbow|Tee|Transition" }].
3) Open Transaction "Pipework::AddValveSet".
4) Split pipe at station. For each entry i, split the right pipe at offset and insert fitting.
5) If any step fails, rollback + WireError.
6) Emit inserted_count + fitting ids.

Uses DistanceBetweenServicesCalculator for MPL spacing. Related: MultiServiceDrawerCommand for full interactive draw workflow.
usecase: User wants to place service drops near a wall and immediately draw horizontally. User wants one-click intelligent drop placement with void detection.
notfor: Drawing horizontal pipes only (use MultiServiceDrawerCommand). Not for AI without useDefaults=true.
precondition: Active document with a plan view. At least one PipingSystemType must exist. Ceiling and slab elements should be present for auto-detect to work.
postcondition: Vertical drop pipes created at the click point with MPL spacing. Horizontal pipes drawn from drop endpoints along user-picked path.

desc: Space-aware move.
summary: Space-aware move. Casts six rays per element and steps into the direction with the largest clearance > 500 mm (200 mm step by default). Falls back to axial centring when no direction meets the clearance threshold.
usecase: Bulk "push these services into the nearest breathing room" cleanup.
notfor: Precise alignment (use ElementAlignment*). Absolute vector moves (use ByDumbVector).
precondition: Active document. Document has a non-template 3D view. ≥1 element resolved.
postcondition: Each element translated into detected space. Audit entry + per-element evidence log.
logictree: 1) Resolve element ids + thresholds. 2) Resolve default 3D view. 3) Transaction: per element cast 6 rays, pick best direction or centring axis, MoveElement. 4) Commit, log per-element result. 5) Failure → WireFault + rollback.

Use the C# class name for commands; the MCP tool name for tools; the website slug for everything else.

desc: Command: Places a Soil Vent Pipe (SVP) at a user-selected location.
summary: Command: Places a Soil Vent Pipe (SVP) at a user-selected location. Uses PlaceSVP for core placement logic. Uses MEPBridge.Revit.SheetSetup.Utilities.ListLevels for level fallback. Uses DrawPipe for pipe geometry creation. WORKFLOW: 1. Lists available piping systems via PlaceSVP.GetAvailableSystems 2. Gets current level from active view 3. Matches and sets drainage system type (Soil/SVP) 4. Prompts user to select placement location 5. Draws 3m vertical pipe upward from selected point 6. Draws 300mm extension downward from selected point
usecase: User needs to place a soil vent pipe stack at a specific location. User wants to quickly add an SVP to a bathroom or WC location. User is laying out drainage system with vertical stacks.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one piping system type must exist. User must be in a plan view to select the placement location. A level must exist in the model (used for Z elevation).
postcondition: Two connected vertical pipes are created at the selected location: - Main SVP: 3000mm upward from level elevation - Extension: 300mm downward from level elevation Pipes use the drainage/soil piping system type.
sideeffects: Creates pipe elements in the model that persist after command completion.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continuecheck: pipingSystemTypes.any → continue, else → error "No piping systems"check: activeView.isPlan → getLevel, else → promptForLevelaction: findDrainageSystem → setSoilDrainageActiveaction: promptUserForLocation → getXYPointaction: createUpwardPipe(3000mm) → addToModelaction: createDownwardExtension(300mm) → addToModelresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: InsertionPoint
selection_inputs.prompt: Select a point to place the SVP

config_inputs.name: UpwardLength
config_inputs.description: Height of main SVP in mm

preprompts: Placing a Soil Vent Pipe at your selected location...
Preparing to place your drainage stack...
thinkingsteps: Detecting current level from active view...
successprompts: Done! Drainage stack is ready at the selected location.
SVP created — 3m up, 300mm down from level.

desc: Opens the QA Manager window — a WebView2 + AI Chat split-panel for running error checks, reviewing QA results, navigating clashes, and managing rule sets.
summary: Opens the QA Manager window — a WebView2 + AI Chat split-panel for running error checks, reviewing QA results, navigating clashes, and managing rule sets.
usecase: Run every QA check on the live Revit model, capture 3D images of each finding, and export a native PDF / DOCX / Excel / XML / HTML report — from one window, or by asking the chat "run every QA check and send me a PDF".

successprompts: QA Manager window is now open.

desc: QaManagerGenerateArchitecturalQaReportCommand — auto-generated CMD skeleton.
summary: QaManagerGenerateArchitecturalQaReportCommand — auto-generated CMD skeleton. Fill workflow body per plan §8.
usecase: Deterministic action per the workflow below — User or Bot path.
notfor: Read-only queries (use the matching MCP tool).
precondition: Active document must be open. User has selection or resolvers are filled.
postcondition: Model state changed inside a Transaction. Audit entry recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot selects target/context (resolvers filled from chat input or UI). 2) Validate preconditions; WireError + abort if missing required state. 3) Open Transaction, perform deterministic steps, WireRecoverable on any fallback. 4) Bot → MCP success summary. User → ribbon toast + chat + audit trail. 5) Failure on either path → `ErrorWire.WireFault` + `CentralWarningLogger.LogError` with structured payload.

desc: QaManagerLaunchOnlineModelViewerInWebAppCommand — open the Adelphos web viewer URL in the default browser.
summary: QaManagerLaunchOnlineModelViewerInWebAppCommand — open the Adelphos web viewer URL in the default browser.
usecase: Hand-off from Revit coordinator to web-app field team.
notfor: Rendering the viewer in-process — different workstream.
precondition: Active document; internet connectivity.
postcondition: Shell launches default browser pointing at the viewer URL.
logictree:
1) base_url = Resolver_BaseUrl ?? env MEPBRIDGE_WEB_VIEWER_BASE ?? 'https://app.adelphos.ai/viewer'.
2) Build url = `{base_url}?project={project_id}&clash={clash_id}` trimming nulls.
3) Process.Start with UseShellExecute=true.
4) Emit ok + url.

desc: QaManagerPushIssuesToAutodeskFormaCommand — POST every filtered clash to APS Construction Issues.
summary: QaManagerPushIssuesToAutodeskFormaCommand — POST every filtered clash to APS Construction Issues.
usecase: Hand-off to Forma-based field team.
notfor: Acquiring the OAuth token itself — done by DocumentController.
precondition: APS bearer token + project id supplied; network; ≥ 1 matching clash.
postcondition: 0-N APS issues created; clash rows updated with issue id via comment append.
logictree:
1) Resolver_BearerToken + Resolver_ProjectId required; WireError otherwise.
2) Pull rows from repo.ListByState(state_filter ?? "active").
3) For each, render title/description via DescriptionTemplateService.
4) await PushClashAsFormaIssue.PushAsync(payload).
5) On ok, append comment "Pushed to Forma as issue {id}" via repo.TransitionState (state unchanged).
6) Aggregate; emit summary.

desc: Generic query command for Claude to call [McpTool] utilities via reflection.
summary: Generic query command for Claude to call [McpTool] utilities via reflection. Uses CommandExecutor parameters to identify the target utility class, locates it by name, invokes its entry method, and serializes the result to JSON. Parameters (via CommandExecutor.GetParameter or JSON): - utility: Class name to call (e.g., "ListLevels", "GetCurrentView", "ListRooms") - method: Method name to call (optional, defaults from [McpTool] EntryMethod or "GetAll"/"Get") - filter: Optional JSON string of parameters to pass to the method
usecase: Claude needs model context data (rooms, levels, views) via the execute_command path. User asks a question about the model and Claude routes through this command.
notfor: Direct MCP tool calls (Claude calls those via GenericContextTool, not this). Model-modifying operations (use a dedicated ExternalCommand instead).
precondition: Active document must be open. The specified utility class must exist and have a compatible static entry method.
postcondition: Query result JSON is stored in LastQueryResult for consumption by the command system. No model modifications are made.

config_inputs.name: utility
config_inputs.description: Class name of the McpTool utility to invoke

config_inputs.name: method
config_inputs.description: Optional method name (defaults to GetAll or Get)

config_inputs.name: filter
config_inputs.description: Optional JSON parameters for the method

desc: Quick Size command - allows rapid resizing of selected MEP elements.
summary: Quick Size command - allows rapid resizing of selected MEP elements. PLACEHOLDER: Implement your sizing logic here. SUGGESTED FEATURES: - Show popup with common sizes - Remember last used size per category - Support batch resizing
usecase: Use when you need to run this command's workflow for the current model task.
notfor: Custom UI required for sizing presets. AI path is JSON-first and dialog-free.
precondition: Active Revit document and required context are available.
postcondition: The command finishes with tracked success or a handled failure state.

desc: Removes the applied view template from a specified view (or the active view).
summary: Removes the applied view template from a specified view (or the active view). Uses SetTemplate to detach the template assignment. Called by Claude when the user wants to free a view from template control.
usecase: User wants to detach a view template so they can manually adjust visibility/graphics. User wants to remove a template before applying a different one.
notfor: Deleting view templates from the project (use Purge or delete directly). Applying a different template (use ApplyViewTemplateCommand instead).
precondition: Active document must be open. Target view must not itself be a template.
postcondition: The view's ViewTemplateId is set to InvalidElementId. The view reverts to manual graphic overrides.

config_inputs.name: viewId
config_inputs.description: Optional target view id (defaults to active view)

desc: Creates a 3D section box tightly fitted around a set of clashing element IDs.
summary: Creates a 3D section box tightly fitted around a set of clashing element IDs. Uses Create3DSectionBox to find the target 3D view and calculate/apply the bounding box with configurable margin. Selects the clash elements after boxing so follow-up commands can target them.
usecase: User wants to zoom into a set of clashing elements to inspect them in 3D. Clash manager highlights a clash group and user wants a focused 3D view.
notfor: Creating section boxes around arbitrary points (use manual section box instead). Running clash detection (use clash detection command first to get element IDs).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one valid element ID must be provided or selected. A non-template 3D view must exist in the document.
postcondition: The active 3D view has a section box tightly enclosing the clashing elements. The clashing elements are selected in the UI.

desc: Selects ALL instances of a family (and optionally a specific type) across the entire model.
summary: Selects ALL instances of a family (and optionally a specific type) across the entire model. If no parameters given, uses the first selected FamilyInstance to determine family/type. Uses CommandExecutor to read parameters from Claude. Uses FilteredElementCollector to scan the entire project. Related: SelectByCategoryCommand for category-wide selection. Related: FilterSelectionCommand for narrowing an existing selection.
usecase: User selects one socket and says "select all of these in the model". User wants to count all instances of a specific family type across all levels. User wants to bulk-edit all instances of a specific family.
notfor: Selecting in current view only (use SelectByFamilyNameCommand with scope=view). Category-wide selection (use SelectByCategoryCommand). Filtering an existing selection (use FilterSelectionCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open. Either familyName parameter must be provided, or user must have a FamilyInstance selected.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with ALL instances of the matching family/type in the entire model. Returns JSON with totalSelected and breakdown by level and type.
logictree: check: parameters.familyName → use directlycheck: selection has FamilyInstance → derive family/type from itcheck: parameters.typeName → if set, match type too (not just family)default: match family name only (all types)

config_inputs.name: familyName
config_inputs.description: Family name to match (or leave empty to use current selection)

config_inputs.name: typeName
config_inputs.description: Optional type name for exact match

preprompts: Selecting all matching instances in the model...
Finding every instance of this family...
successprompts: Selected {totalSelected} instances of {familyName} across the model.

desc: Selects all titleblock instances in the project.
summary: Selects all titleblock instances in the project. Uses FilteredElementCollector to find all OST_TitleBlocks elements. Uses CommandExecutor for result reporting. Related: SelectByCategoryCommand for generic category selection. Related: SelectAllInstancesCommand for family-specific selection.
usecase: User wants to select all titleblocks to verify they are the correct type. User wants to bulk-edit titleblock parameters (project name, number, etc.). User wants to audit which sheets have titleblocks.
notfor: Selecting a specific titleblock family (use SelectAllInstancesCommand with familyName). Selecting sheets (titleblocks are not sheets — use a sheet selection command). Editing titleblock type (use a type-swap command).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one sheet with a titleblock must exist.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with all titleblock instances in the project. Returns JSON with success flag, count, and element IDs.
logictree: collect: FilteredElementCollector → OST_TitleBlocks → instances onlyset: UIDocument.Selection → all collected IDsreturn: JSON with count and IDs

preprompts: Selecting all titleblocks in the project...

desc: Selects all elements of a given category in the current view or entire model.
summary: Selects all elements of a given category in the current view or entire model. Maps friendly names to BuiltInCategory enums with fuzzy matching. Uses CommandExecutor to read parameters from Claude. Follow up with FilterSelectionCommand to narrow the selection. Related: SelectAllInstancesCommand for family-specific selection. Related: SelectByFamilyNameCommand for family name selection.
usecase: User wants to select all lighting fixtures to tag them. User wants to count how many pipes are in the current view. User wants to pre-select elements before running TagAllCommand.
notfor: Selecting by family name (use SelectByFamilyNameCommand). Selecting by parameter value (use SelectByParameterCommand). Filtering an existing selection (use FilterSelectionCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid active view.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with all elements matching the category. Returns JSON with totalSelected and breakdown by family.
logictree: check: parameters.category → resolve category name to BuiltInCategorycheck: parameters.allViews == "true" → search entire modeldefault: search current view only

config_inputs.name: allViews
config_inputs.description: Search entire model instead of current view

outputs.name: Summary
outputs.description: Breakdown by family name and count

preprompts: Finding all elements in the category...

desc: Selects elements by family name, type name, or element name (contains match).
summary: Selects elements by family name, type name, or element name (contains match). Uses FilteredElementCollector to gather elements, then filters by case-insensitive search term. Optionally narrows via SelectByCategoryCommand.TryResolveCategory. Called by CommandExecutor when Claude invokes execute_command. Related: SelectByParameterCommand for deep parameter search, SelectByCategoryCommand for category-only selection.
usecase: User wants to select all downlights (search="downlight"). User wants to select all EDC_LF_ families (search="EDC_LF"). User wants to find elements of a specific type across the model.
notfor: Selecting by category (use SelectByCategoryCommand — faster). Deep parameter value search (use SelectByParameterCommand). Filtering an existing selection (use FilterSelectionCommand).
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid active view.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with all elements whose family/type/element name contains the search term. Returns JSON with totalSelected, totalSearched, and breakdown by family:type.
logictree: check: parameters.search → required, fail if emptycheck: parameters.category → optional, narrows collector for performancecheck: parameters.scope == "model" → search entire modeldefault: search current view only

config_inputs.name: search
config_inputs.description: Text to search for in family/type/element name

Looking for families matching your search term...

desc: Selects elements matching a named ParameterFilterElement.
summary: Selects elements matching a named ParameterFilterElement. Resolves the filter by name, extracts categories via GetCategories() and rules via GetElementFilter(), then applies to all elements in the chosen scope. Uses SelectByFamilyNameCommand.BuildFamilyTypeBreakdown for result summary. Called by CommandExecutor when Claude invokes execute_command. Related: SelectByCategoryCommand for category-only selection, SelectByParameterCommand for deep parameter search.
usecase: Select all elements currently targeted by "Coordination - Clash" filter. Select elements matching a discipline-specific filter before export. Verify which elements a view filter affects before toggling visibility.
notfor: Creating or editing filters (manual Revit operation). Selecting by category alone (use SelectByCategoryCommand — faster). Deep parameter search across all fields (use SelectByParameterCommand).
precondition: Document must contain at least one ParameterFilterElement. Active document and view must be open.
postcondition: Selection is replaced with elements that pass the filter rule set. Returns JSON with filterName, scope, totalSearched, totalSelected, and breakdown.
logictree: check: parameters.filterName → required, fail if emptycheck: resolve ParameterFilterElement by name → fail if not found (list available)check: parameters.scope == "model" → search entire modeldefault: search current view only

config_inputs.name: filterName
config_inputs.description: Name of the ParameterFilterElement to use

desc: Sets the Revit selection to the given element IDs.
summary: Sets the Revit selection to the given element IDs. Called by MEPBridge.Revit.AIChat.Services.CustomTools.SelectByIdsTool via execute_command. Related: SelectByLocationCommand for coordinate-based selection, SelectByRoomCommand for room-based selection.
usecase: Cross-off workflow: list rads → get IDs → select_by_ids(one id) → act → next.
notfor: Selecting by category/family/room — use SelectByRoomCommand or other selection commands first, then this with their IDs.
precondition: Active document; elementIds = JSON array [1,2,3] or comma-separated numbers.
postcondition: Selection is set to valid IDs; result returns success and count.

desc: Returns elements at or near the given X/Y/Z coordinates (mm).
summary: Returns elements at or near the given X/Y/Z coordinates (mm). Optionally sets Revit selection to those elements. Uses FilteredElementCollector to find elements within a tolerance radius. Called by MEPBridge.Revit.AIChat.Services.CustomTools.SelectByLocationTool via execute_command. Related: SelectByIdsCommand for ID-based selection, SelectByRoomCommand for room-based selection.
usecase: Find/select elements by coordinates; then use SelectByIdsCommand for cross-off.
notfor: Selecting by category/family/room — use SelectByRoomCommand. Selecting by ID — use SelectByIdsCommand.
precondition: Active document; xMm, yMm, zMm required (numbers in mm).
postcondition: Returns { success, count, positionMm, items }; optionally selection set.

desc: Selects elements where ANY parameter value contains the search term.
summary: Selects elements where ANY parameter value contains the search term. Uses SearchParameterValue.Contains() which checks element name, family name, type name, all instance parameters, and all type parameters. Optionally narrows by category via SelectByCategoryCommand.TryResolveCategory. Uses SelectByFamilyNameCommand.BuildFamilyTypeBreakdown for result summary. Called by CommandExecutor when Claude invokes execute_command. Related: SelectByFamilyNameCommand for faster name-only search, SelectByCategoryCommand for category-only selection.
usecase: User wants to find all elements marked "emergency" in any parameter. User wants to select elements with a specific circuit name or system name. User wants to find elements by a value buried in type parameters.
notfor: Simple category selection (use SelectByCategoryCommand — much faster). Family name search (use SelectByFamilyNameCommand — faster).
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid active view.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with elements where at least one parameter contains the search text. Returns JSON with totalSelected, totalSearched, and breakdown. NOTE: Slower than SelectByCategoryCommand/SelectByFamilyNameCommand — searches ALL parameters.
logictree: check: parameters.search → required, fail if emptycheck: parameters.category → optional, narrows collector for performancecheck: parameters.scope == "model" → search entire modeldefault: search current view only

config_inputs.description: Text to search for in ALL parameter values

desc: Selects elements where service type style parameters match provided text.
summary: Selects elements where service type style parameters match provided text. Checks multiple parameter name variants: "Service Type", "System Type", "System-Type", "ServiceType", "SystemType" — covering different MEP disciplines. Uses SelectByFamilyNameCommand.BuildFamilyTypeBreakdown for result summary. Called by CommandExecutor when Claude invokes execute_command. Related: SelectBySystemTypeCommand for MEP system classification search, SelectByParameterCommand for generic deep parameter search.
usecase: Select all CHW (chilled water) services by Service Type parameter value. Select all DHW (domestic hot water) elements for tagging. Select all Lighting services before running a lighting calculation.
notfor: Generic deep parameter search across all fields (use SelectByParameterCommand). Searching by Revit MEP system classification (use SelectBySystemTypeCommand). Simple category selection (use SelectByCategoryCommand).
precondition: Active document and view must be available. Elements in scope must have service type parameters populated.
postcondition: Selection is replaced with matching element IDs. Returns JSON with serviceType, scope, totalSearched, totalSelected, and breakdown.
logictree: check: parameters.serviceType → required, fail if emptycheck: parameters.scope == "model" → search entire modeldefault: search current view onlyiterate: each element → check "Service Type" / "System Type" params for contains match

Related: SelectByServiceTypeCommand for custom "Service Type" parameter search, SelectByParameterCommand for generic deep parameter search.
usecase: Select all Domestic Hot Water elements before tagging. Select all Supply Air duct elements for a coordination check. Select all elements in a specific electrical circuit type.
notfor: Selecting by custom "Service Type" parameter (use SelectByServiceTypeCommand). Generic deep parameter search across all fields (use SelectByParameterCommand). Simple category selection (use SelectByCategoryCommand).

postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with matched element IDs. Returns JSON with systemType, scope, totalSearched, totalSelected, and breakdown.

desc: Selects all model elements that belong to a named workset.
summary: Selects all model elements that belong to a named workset. Uses ListWorksets to resolve the workset by name, then applies ElementWorksetFilter to a collector scoped to the active view or the whole model. Called by CommandExecutor when triggered from AI chat. Related: SelectByCategoryCommand for category-based selection.
usecase: User says "select all elements on the drainage workset". User wants to isolate a specific workset's elements for review. User needs to count or inspect elements assigned to a workset.
notfor: Changing workset assignments (use MoveToWorksetCommand). Creating or deleting worksets. Selecting by category (use SelectByCategoryCommand).
precondition: Document must be workshared. Target workset must exist.
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with elements from the matching workset. Returns JSON summary with count and scope.

desc: Select Connected command - selects all elements connected to current selection.
summary: Select Connected command - selects all elements connected to current selection. Traverses MEP connectors to build the complete connected network. FEATURES: - Traverses connector relationships - Works with ducts, pipes, cable trays, conduits - Includes fittings and accessories
usecase: Use when you need to run this command's workflow for the current model task.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active Revit document and required context are available.
postcondition: The command finishes with tracked success or a handled failure state.

desc: Selects elements visible in the current canvas viewport (what the user sees on screen).
summary: Selects elements visible in the current canvas viewport (what the user sees on screen). Uses the view's crop box or visible corners to scope the selection spatially.
usecase: User is zoomed into a corridor and says "select all lights here". User wants to act on only the visible portion of a large floor plan. User wants to select elements "on screen" not the whole view.
notfor: Selecting across entire view (use SelectByCategoryCommand with view scope). Selecting across entire model (use SelectByCategoryCommand with allViews=true).
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid active view (floor plan, ceiling plan, or section).
postcondition: Revit selection is replaced with elements visible in the canvas viewport. Returns JSON with totalSelected and breakdown.
logictree: check: view.CropBoxActive → use crop box boundsfallback: use UIView.GetZoomCorners() for actual viewport boundsfilter: optional category parameter to narrow results

desc: Selects titleblock instances on a sheet (active sheet by default).
summary: Selects titleblock instances on a sheet (active sheet by default).
usecase: Use when you need to run this command's workflow for the current model task.
notfor: Not for bypassing model validation rules or replacing manual design judgment.
precondition: Active Revit document and required context are available.
postcondition: The command finishes with tracked success or a handled failure state.

config_inputs.name: sheetNumber
config_inputs.description: Optional target sheet number. Defaults to active sheet view.

desc: Allows user to draw a rectangle corner-to-corner to set as the view's crop region.
summary: Allows user to draw a rectangle corner-to-corner to set as the view's crop region. Uses SetCropView.FromCorners for the crop region geometry calculation. Only works on plan, section, and elevation views — not on sheets, schedules, or 3D views.
usecase: User wants to define a custom crop region on a plan or section view for sheet placement.
notfor: Cropping 3D views or sheets. Resetting crop to default. Scope box assignment (use SetViewToScopeBoxCommand).
precondition: Active view must be a plan, section, or elevation view (not sheet, schedule, or 3D).
postcondition: View crop region is set to the rectangle defined by the two picked corners. Crop is enabled.

preprompts: Setting the crop region from picked corners...
successprompts: Done — view is now cropped to your rectangle.

desc: Sets the template .rvt file path in the project/user/business config.
summary: Sets the template .rvt file path in the project/user/business config. Validates file exists before saving. Uses TransferStandardsConfigResolver for three-tier config persistence.
usecase: User wants to configure which template file to use for transfer standards.
notfor: Performing actual transfers (use TransferAllStandardsFromSourceByCategory).
precondition: Active Revit document must be open.
postcondition: Template path saved to the appropriate config tier.

desc: Sets the current view to use a selected scope box for its crop region, or removes the existing scope box assignment.
summary: Sets the current view to use a selected scope box for its crop region, or removes the existing scope box assignment. Resolves scope box by ID, name, or auto-selects when only one exists. Uses SetViewToScopeBox for assignment/removal logic and ListScopeBoxes to enumerate available scope boxes. Related: SetCropViewCommand for manual crop rectangles.
usecase: User wants to align a view's crop to a named scope box for consistent sheet output.
notfor: Manual crop rectangles (use SetCropViewCommand). Creating new scope boxes.
precondition: Active view must support scope box assignment (plan, section, elevation — not sheet or schedule).
postcondition: View crop region is controlled by the assigned scope box, or scope box is removed.

config_inputs.name: scopeBoxId
config_inputs.description: Element ID of the scope box to assign

config_inputs.name: scopeBoxName
config_inputs.description: Name of the scope box to assign

config_inputs.name: removeScopeBox
config_inputs.description: If true, removes the current scope box

preprompts: Assigning scope box to the current view...
Setting up scope box for this view...
successprompts: Scope box '{ScopeBoxName}' applied to the view.
Done — view is now using the selected scope box.

desc: Main orchestrator class that coordinates the entire sheet setup workflow.
summary: Main orchestrator class that coordinates the entire sheet setup workflow. This is the entry point that sequences all operations from model analysis through sheet creation. THIN ORCHESTRATOR PATTERN: - Contains NO logic - just calls helpers in sequence - All auto-determination is done BY the helpers internally - Only interaction point: ServiceSelectionDialog for user to select services
usecase: Use when you need to run this command's workflow for the current model task.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active Revit document and required context are available.
postcondition: The command finishes with tracked success or a handled failure state.

desc: Create N (4/8) planning elevations orbiting the building BB centre.
summary: Create N (4/8) planning elevations orbiting the building BB centre.

desc: Main Entry Point: Orchestrates the entire MEP sheet setup workflow.
summary: Main Entry Point: Orchestrates the entire MEP sheet setup workflow. This command automates the creation of sheets and views for MEP documentation: - Auto-detects primary levels (filters duplicates) - Auto-detects existing sheets and views (reuses them) - Auto-infers sheet naming patterns from existing sheets - Auto-selects titleblocks based on view sizes - Auto-applies view templates based on service type - Creates scope boxes for plant rooms and risers if needed - Creates sheets with proper layouts for plans, elevations, and sections ONLY USER INTERACTION: ServiceSelectionDialog to select which MEP services to document.
usecase: User wants to set up all MEP documentation sheets automatically. User has a new project and needs to create all standard MEP sheets. User wants to add sheets for additional MEP services to an existing project.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open with MEP elements. At least one level must exist in the model. Titleblocks must be loaded in the project.
postcondition: Sheets are created for each selected service type. Views are created and placed on sheets with appropriate layouts. Statistics are displayed showing what was created/reused.
logictree: check: document.isValid → proceedcheck: levels.count > 0 → proceedcheck: titleblocks.count > 0 → proceedaction: detectServices → showServiceSelectionDialogaction: userSelectsServices → createSheetsAndViews

desc: Creates a Detail callout inside the active plan view at a supplied (min, max) bounding box.
summary: Creates a Detail callout inside the active plan view at a supplied (min, max) bounding box. Pure maths delegated to GetCalloutRectangle. View-family-type lookup via ResolveDetailViewFamilyTypeId.
usecase: Frame an arbitrary region for a detail drawing or evidence capture.
notfor: Frame-a-room callouts (use SheetViewSetupCreateCalloutWrappingRoomOrSpaceBoundaryCommand).
precondition: Active document open; active view is a plan/section; Project contains at least one Detail ViewFamilyType; BoundingBoxXYZ supplied via resolvers is valid (min < max on X and Y).
postcondition: Exactly one new callout View added as a child of the parent view, inside a single committed Transaction, audit trail recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies Resolver_ParentViewId (defaults to active view) + Resolver_MinX/Y/Z + Resolver_MaxX/Y/Z in feet. 2) Validate — parent view exists and supports callouts; min < max on X and Y; else WireError + abort. 3) Resolve Detail ViewFamilyType id via ResolveDetailViewFamilyTypeId.FindFirstInDocument(doc); if invalid WireError + abort. 4) Open Transaction 'Create callout (bbox)'. 5) Build rect via GetCalloutRectangle.FromBoundingBox(bb). 6) callout = ViewSection.CreateCallout(doc, parentViewId, vftId, rect.min, rect.max); commit. 7) Bot: JSON {ok, callout_view_id, parent_view_id, min, max}. User: ribbon toast 'Callout placed in {parentView.Name}.' 8) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {parent_view_id, min, max}.

preprompts: Creating callout in parent view at supplied bounding box…

desc: Creates a Detail callout view that wraps a selected Room or Space with a configurable margin.
summary: Creates a Detail callout view that wraps a selected Room or Space with a configurable margin. Pure maths delegated to GetCalloutRectangle.
usecase: Detail callout around a semantic room or MEP space.
notfor: Arbitrary region callouts (use SheetViewSetupCreateCalloutOnViewAtBoundingBoxCommand).
precondition: Active document open; parent view supports callouts; Room/Space id resolves; project has a Detail ViewFamilyType.
postcondition: One new callout view added, inside one committed Transaction, audit trail recorded.
logictree: 1) User or Bot supplies Resolver_ParentViewId (defaults to active view) + Resolver_RoomOrSpaceId + Resolver_MarginMm (default 600). 2) Resolve the element; if it is neither a Room nor a Space WireError + abort. 3) Resolve Detail ViewFamilyType id via ResolveDetailViewFamilyTypeId.FindFirstInDocument; if invalid WireError + abort. 4) Open Transaction 'Create callout (room/space wrap)'. 5) rect = GetCalloutRectangle.FromRoom(room, marginFt) OR FromSpace(space, marginFt). 6) callout = ViewSection.CreateCallout(doc, parentView.Id, vftId, rect.min, rect.max); commit. 7) Bot: JSON {ok, callout_view_id, parent_view_id, room_or_space_id, margin_mm}. User: toast 'Callout placed around {target.Name}.' 8) Any exception → tracker.Fail(ex); WireFault {parent_view_id, room_or_space_id, margin_mm}.
