desc: Opens or toggles the BUILD X AI Chat panel inside Revit.
summary: Opens or toggles the BUILD X AI Chat panel inside Revit. On first invocation it bootstraps CommandExecutor and registers the RevitContextBridge so that MCP tools (get_selection, get_parameters, etc.) become available to Claude. Subsequent calls simply toggle the panel visibility. Prefers a modeless WPF window during Add-in Manager development; otherwise uses the Revit dockable pane.
usecase: Click the BUILD X ribbon button to open the AI assistant.
notfor: Not for headless/batch automation — requires an active Revit UI session.
precondition: Revit is running with a valid UIApplication context.
postcondition: The AI Chat panel is visible and the RevitContextBridge is registered.

preprompts: Opening the BUILD X chat panel…

desc: Exports the active Revit model (or a scoped subset) to a local SQLite snapshot database.
summary: Exports the active Revit model (or a scoped subset) to a local SQLite snapshot database. Runs on the Revit thread via CommandExecutor to avoid Microsoft.Data.Sqlite assembly resolution failures that occur when snapshot_export is called directly from MCP. Uses ModelSnapshot for data extraction and SnapshotDatabase for storage. Sets SnapshotRegistry.Current on success so downstream query tools can read the snapshot.

desc: External command that queries the SQLite model snapshot.
summary: External command that queries the SQLite model snapshot. Runs in Add-In Manager ALC context so Microsoft.Data.Sqlite resolves correctly. Requires a prior export via SnapshotExportCommand. Tools.MCPSnapshotQuery routes here via execute_command. Uses SnapshotDatabase.QueryAsJson for raw SQL and structured queries. Uses SnapshotRegistry to retrieve the current snapshot instance.
usecase: User wants to query elements by category, level, or parameter value from the snapshot. User wants to run raw SQL joins across elements and parameters tables. User wants snapshot metadata (element counts, export time, scope). AI sub-agents need fast parallel reads without hitting the Revit thread.
notfor: Exporting/creating a snapshot (use SnapshotExportCommand instead). Writing parameter changes (use snapshot_write/snapshot_apply instead). Live Revit model queries that need real-time data (use MCP context tools instead).
precondition: A snapshot must have been exported via SnapshotExportCommand (snapshot_export). The SnapshotRegistry.Current must be non-null and IsReady.
postcondition: Returns JSON query results via CommandExecutor.SetResultObject. No model changes — read-only operation on the SQLite database.
logictree: check: info == "true" → return snapshot metadatacheck: sql provided → validate read-only → execute raw SQLcheck: table provided → build structured query with filters → executefallback: → return snapshot metadata (info)

desc: Snaps selected family instances to the nearest wall face.

After importing blocks via ImportCommand, wall-mounted items may be placed at the correct XY/Z but not snapped to the wall face — this command fixes that. Uses ObtainRoomBoundaryWalls to resolve room boundary walls. Uses ElementTransformUtils for move and rotate operations.

notfor: Snapping elements to floors, ceilings, or other surfaces. Not for elements that aren't family instances. Not for creating new elements.

postcondition: Selected family instances moved to nearest wall face and rotated to face wall normal. Z height unchanged.

desc: Tag All command - adds tags to all selected MEP elements using intelligent placement.
summary: Tag All command - adds tags to all selected MEP elements using intelligent placement. FEATURES: - Category-specific tag placement rules - Vertical elements (risers/drops) tagged 12.7mm left/right based on room centroid - Horizontal elements tagged at midpoint with no leader - Grilles and other elements use intelligent clutter detection - Mechanical equipment tagged at center with no leader - Fittings are not tagged TAG RULES: | Category | Behavior | |-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | SVP/Sanitary Pipes | Horizontal only, >500mm, collision-free, 1000mm clusters | | Vertical Ducts/Pipes/Trays | 12.7mm offset L/R based on room centroid | | Horizontal Ducts/Pipes/Trays| Midpoint, no leader | | Short segments (<250mm) | Not tagged | | Grilles (Duct Terminals) | Intelligent: least cluttered direction | | Mechanical Equipment | Center, no leader | | Accessories/Sprinklers/etc | Intelligent: least cluttered direction | | Fittings | Not tagged |
usecase: Use when you need to run this command's workflow for the current model task.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active Revit document and required context are available.
postcondition: The command finishes with tracked success or a handled failure state.

desc: Phase 8 Command: Tags all rooms in the current view.
summary: Phase 8 Command: Tags all rooms in the current view. Supports rooms from both host document and linked models. Uses room tag families filtered by "room" keyword.
usecase: User wants to tag all rooms in a floor plan quickly. User needs to add room tags to rooms from linked architectural model. User wants to verify all rooms are properly tagged before issue.
notfor: Custom UI required for rich tag-type picker. AI path is JSON-first and dialog-free.
precondition: Active document must be open with a valid plan or section view active. At least one room must exist in the model (host or linked). At least one room tag family must be loaded in the document. View must support annotation (not 3D or schedules).
postcondition: All visible rooms in the current view are tagged with room tags. Rooms from linked models are tagged using room tags in the host document. Tags are placed at room center points. Summary dialog shows count of tags created and any failures.
logictree: check: activeView.isValidForAnnotation → proceedcheck: activeView.is3D || activeView.isSchedule → fail "Cannot tag in this view type"check: loadedRoomTags.count == 0 → fail "No room tag families loaded"check: visibleRooms.count == 0 → fail "No rooms visible in current view"check: selectedTagType != null → useSelectedTagTypecheck: selectedTagType == null → useFirstAvailableTagTyperesolve: includeLinkedRooms == true → GetAllIncludingLinks, else → GetAll

desc: Debug command that visualizes corridor width measurement from doors.
summary: Debug command that visualizes corridor width measurement from doors. User selects a room, then rays are drawn from each door into the room to measure distance to the opposite wall. Draws detail curves in the active plan view: lines for rays, circles at hit points, crosses at doors. Uses ListDoors.GetDoorsForRoom to find doors for the room.
usecase: User wants to debug corridor width measurement logic. User needs to verify door-to-wall distance calculations visually. User wants to classify a room as corridor vs circulation based on width.
notfor: Not for production documentation — debug visualization only. Not for creating permanent annotations on sheets. Not for autonomous AI execution — requires interactive room selection.
precondition: Active view must be a plan view (Floor Plan, Ceiling Plan, Engineering Plan, or Area Plan). At least one room must exist in the model for selection. The room must have at least one door for ray visualization.
postcondition: Detail curves are drawn in the active view showing door-to-wall rays. Cross markers appear at door locations, circles at wall hit points. Summary dialog shows measurements and corridor classification.
logictree: check: activeView.isPlanView → proceedcheck: activeView.isNotPlan → fail "Run from a Floor Plan view"action: userPicksRoom → getRoomDoorscheck: doors.count == 0 → report "No doors found"action: fireRaysFromDoors → drawVisualization → reportMeasurements

selection_inputs.description: Room to visualize door rays for
selection_inputs.prompt: Select a room to visualize door rays

thinkingsteps: Firing rays from doors to opposite walls...
successprompts: Door ray visualization complete — {RaysDrawn} rays drawn.

desc: VoxelGridBuildForScopeAtResolutionCommand — build + obstruction- stamp + cache a VoxelGrid for subsequent A* queries.
summary: VoxelGridBuildForScopeAtResolutionCommand — build + obstruction- stamp + cache a VoxelGrid for subsequent A* queries.
usecase: Prime the cache before a routing run.
notfor: Actual routing — use the Route command.
precondition: Scope resolvable (scope_box / room / min-max).
postcondition: In-process voxel cache updated; no Revit mutation.
logictree:
1) Resolve scope from resolvers. WireError if none.
2) BuildVoxelGridForVolume.BuildFrom* with cell size.
3) StampObstructionsOntoVoxelGrid.StampFromDocument to mark obstructed cells.
4) Register in VoxelGridCache keyed by scope signature.
5) Emit grid metadata.

desc: VoxelGridRoutePipeOrDuctBetweenConnectorsCommand — voxel A* + placeholder emission in one Transaction.
summary: VoxelGridRoutePipeOrDuctBetweenConnectorsCommand — voxel A* + placeholder emission in one Transaction.
usecase: Voxel-guided routing.
notfor: Multi-service rack — use RouteMultiServiceRack.
precondition: Voxel grid cached for scope; start/end points reachable; system + type + level resolvable.
postcondition: One or more placeholder pipe/duct segments inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve cached grid from scope_tag. WireError if not cached.
2) Snap start/end to cells.
3) Run A* (allow_45).
4) If not found → WireError + emit reason.
5) Open Transaction; emit pipe or duct placeholders via service; commit.
6) Emit placeholder ids + cost + path length.

desc: VoxelGridRouteMultiServiceRackThroughCorridorCommand — sequential rack routing: each service inflates the cache's Forbidden zones before the next A* runs so services stack without overlap.
summary: VoxelGridRouteMultiServiceRackThroughCorridorCommand — sequential rack routing: each service inflates the cache's Forbidden zones before the next A* runs so services stack without overlap.
usecase: Corridor multi-service stacking.
notfor: Single service — use the single-service command.
precondition: Voxel grid cached; services list non-empty.
postcondition: 0..N placeholders per service, all inside one Transaction.
logictree:
1) Resolve cached grid.
2) For each service in order: (a) inflate clearance zones of prior services, (b) A*, (c) emit placeholder segments.
3) If any service fails, rollback whole Transaction.
4) Emit per-service results + totals.

thinkingsteps: Running A* per service with cumulative clearance…
